programme.title: My Cat from Hell

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy investigates the behaviour of cat Einstein, which is mild until it sees fellow feline Izzy - and then goes on the attack. Plus, an expectant couple worry about their pet's mood swings

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: Warden Bailey responds to a call about underage drinkers holding a fishing tournament. Elsewhere, warden Hensz handles an aggressive family of owls

programme.desc: Dr Jeff helps a beloved German shepherd walk again, while Dr Don has to act fast when a cat eats poison and a scared dog runs loose outside the clinic

programme.desc: Determined survivalists the Kilchers return, and with the family legacy at risk, they work to revamp the homestead. However, a huge snowstorm threatens their work

programme.title: Alaskan Bush People
programme.desc: The Browns are ready to return to the wild after being stuck in Ketchikan, but a series of emergencies threaten to keep them trapped in modern society

programme.title: Mountain Monsters

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Jackson Galaxy enters the Santé D'or shelter with the intention of helping an over-aggressive shelter cat who goes by the name of Pig Pen

programme.title: Meet the Orangutans
programme.desc: Chiquita was only two months old when she arrived at Sepilok sanctuary. Now aged three, her surrogate attachment to one of the staff is causing developmental issues

programme.title: Gluck: Who Did She Think He Was?
programme.desc: The untold story of Britain's 1930s cross-dressing high society painter, whose shows were attended by royalty, aristocrats and celebrities, dressed as a man, and called her exhibitions 'one-man shows'. All of Gluck's lovers were women, including flower arranger to the stars Constance Spry, and Edith Heald, the ex-mistress of WB Yeats

programme.title: Margot Fonteyn
programme.desc: A tribute to the ballerina, first broadcast shortly after her death in 1991, with interviews with colleagues and friends and extracts from ballets from throughout her career

programme.title: Art That Made Us
programme.desc: The world wars force artists to grapple with destruction and savagery, challenging conventions and the old imperial order, while those fleeing war bring new ideas to Britain. Contemporary war photographer Oliver Chanarin traces the story of William Orpen's subversive protest image, To the Unknown British Soldier in France, picturing a lone draped coffin amid the magnificence of the Palace of Versailles where peace delegates met in 1919, while artist Ryan Gander examines how sculptor Barbara Hepworth tried to make sense of war by reaching for beauty in abstract human forms and Denzil Forrester looks ahead to the post-colonial aftermath of war

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman asks the questions as two new teams join the competition. Jesus College, Cambridge and St Catherine's College, Oxford are both hoping to secure themselves a place in the second round

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell presents a quiz show of patience and lateral thinking, where successful contestants make connections between things which at first do not appear to be linked. In this episode, the Crossworders do battle with the Edinburgh Scrabblers

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth discovers Emmet has planned a piano concert for the senior citizens at the church hall, and decides to treat the lucky audience to her own brand of sing-song. Suburban comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and David Griffin

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: Timothy, after some initial complications, is asked to be best man at his friend Frank's wedding. He ends up in trouble as a result of trying to encourage the idea of marriage in spite of his parents

programme.title: A History of Britain by Simon Schama
programme.desc: Simon Schama continues his journey through British history, discovering how the expansion of imperialism was built on the Empire's slave trade and relied on the subjugation of native peoples to enrich the mother country. This was despite Britons' natural distrust of large armies and all-powerful governments

programme.title: The Corinthians: We Were The Champions
programme.desc: The incredible real-life story of the original rebel girls of football, Corinthians Ladies FC from Manchester, who defied the English Football Association's 50-year ban on women playing to become global champions. Told exclusively by ten of the surviving players in their own words

programme.title: Berlusconi: Condemned to Win
programme.desc: A look at how Silvio Berlusconi managed to dodge corruption charges, win again with Milan and blur the line between football and politics, all while controversy and his cult grew

programme.title: Andrew Davies: Rewriting the Classics
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the creative process of the screenwriter behind some of TV's most acclaimed adaptations of classic literature, from the 1995 version of Pride and Prejudice to the 2018 dramatisation of Victor Hugo's Les Miserables. He also discusses how to go about transforming sprawling, lengthy and detailed novels into primetime drama, turning one art form into another altogether different one. With contributions from authors Sarah Waters and Helen Fielding, actors Dominic West and Olivia Colman, and industry figures Jane Tranter and Sue Birtwhistle

programme.title: An Art Lovers' Guide
programme.desc: Janina Ramirez and Alastair Sooke explore the capital of Azerbaijan, which offers a mix of the ancient and modern in a country where cultural life is tightly controlled

programme.title: India: Nature's Wonderland
programme.desc: Part one of two. Wildlife expert Liz Bonnin, Slumdog Millionaire actress Freida Pinto and mountaineer Jon Gupta explore the hidden wonders of the natural world in India, a land where tigers dance and lions roam. This edition starts with Liz visiting the Gir Forest, home to the world's last Asiatic lions, where she witness two females hunting, before watching elephants walking through a tea plantation. Freida meets a man who has dedicated his life to the hoolock gibbon, India's only ape, which starts the morning by singing. Jon climbs into the Himalayas and stops off at Debprayag to bathe in the sacred waters at a holy place where the River Ganges begins

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman asks the questions as Balliol College, Oxford takes on the University of Southampton, with both teams hoping to secure themselves a place in the second round

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell introduces this quarter-final of the quiz show, in which the Bankers face the IT Supporters in a test of patience and lateral thinking, hoping to make connections between things which at first do not appear to be linked

programme.title: From Ice to Fire: The Incredible Science of Temperature
programme.desc: Physicist Dr Helen Czerski explores the narrow band of temperature that has led to life on Earth. She reveals how life began in a place where hot meets cold, and how every living creature on Earth depends on temperature for its survival. She uncovers the extraordinary natural engineering that animals have evolved to keep their bodies at the right temperature and she witnesses the remarkable surgery that is using temperature to take the human body to the extremes

programme.title: The Ruth Ellis Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: Gillian Pachter focuses on the three weeks leading up to Ruth Ellis's execution, and the last-minute attempts to save her life even though Ruth herself was determined to die. The killer finally admitted she had an accomplice, prompting her solicitor to race to the Home Office in a bid to stop the execution. But it was all in vain and Ruth was hanged. Gillian goes on to explore the role of her case in the introduction of the defence of diminished responsibility in England and its place in the eventual abolition of capital punishment in 1965

programme.title: Andrew Davies Remembers - A Very Peculiar Practice
programme.desc: Screenwriter Andrew Davies, best known for his adaptations of classic novels, reflects on one of his own original works - the 1980s black comedy, A Very Peculiar Practice. Inspired by his own experiences in formal education, the series drew heavily on the political landscape of the time. Davies discusses the show's origins, its cult status and how its dark humour paved the way for his later work

programme.title: A Very Peculiar Practice
programme.sub-title: A Very Long Way from Anywhere
programme.desc: An idealistic doctor starts work at a university practice, but is unprepared for the back-stabbing antics of the lecturers and his colleagues. Peter Davison stars in the first episode of Andrew Davies' surreal campus drama

programme.title: A Very Peculiar Practice
programme.sub-title: We Love You, That's Why We're Here
programme.desc: Steven gives a speech to the new students, and becomes involved in the problems of two freshers - a religious fanatic who's determined to preserve her virginity, and a liberated lass who's desperate to give it away. Peter Davison and Francesca Brill star

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Sylvia Syms explores the handsome heroes and pin-ups of the silver screen, including Paul Newman, Robert Redford, Jane Russell and Marilyn Monroe. Plus, hearing from some of the stars themselves talking about what their sex symbol status meant to them

programme.title: Eve and Marilyn
programme.desc: Eve Arnold, one of the world's leading photo-journalists, remembers photographing Marilyn Monroe over the ten crucial years of her stardom and decline. Going back to the original contact sheets and colour material, Arnold tells of their friendship and what it was like as a woman to photograph Marilyn Monroe. First broadcast September 8, 1987

programme.title: From Ice to Fire: The Incredible Science of Temperature
programme.desc: Physicist Dr Helen Czerski explores the narrow band of temperature that has led to life on Earth. She reveals how life began in a place where hot meets cold, and how every living creature on Earth depends on temperature for its survival. She uncovers the extraordinary natural engineering that animals have evolved to keep their bodies at the right temperature and she witnesses the remarkable surgery that is using temperature to take the human body to the extremes

programme.title: The Ruth Ellis Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: Gillian Pachter focuses on the three weeks leading up to Ruth Ellis's execution, and the last-minute attempts to save her life even though Ruth herself was determined to die. The killer finally admitted she had an accomplice, prompting her solicitor to race to the Home Office in a bid to stop the execution. But it was all in vain and Ruth was hanged. Gillian goes on to explore the role of her case in the introduction of the defence of diminished responsibility in England and its place in the eventual abolition of capital punishment in 1965

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.sub-title: My Timeshare Is Worth Less Than When I Bought It
programme.desc: The team investigate the holiday timeshare company that members feel has moved the goalposts as they reached the end of their contracts - with their investments worth far less than they had expected. There's also advice for the couple who cancelled their Caribbean trip due to devastating hurricanes only to be charged every penny by their travel company

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.sub-title: Good Things Come in Small Packages
programme.desc: Martin Roberts contemplates whether a house in south Wales is the smallest he has ever encountered in his 20 years on the programme. Dion Dublin is captivated by a rather funky, retro carpet in a terrace in Lancashire, and Tommy Walsh is in Wandsworth, south London, where a one-bedroom garden flat has potential to add space

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and the teams head to an antiques fair in Builth Wells in mid-Wales. Experts Ben Cooper and Kate Bliss are on hand to help the reds and blues pick the best bargains to take to auction, and Natasha pays a visit to a special museum in Kidderminster to learn about the history of carpet-making in the Worcestershire town

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show 2026
programme.desc: It's Medals Day at Chelsea 2026, where Angellica Bell and Nicki Chapman celebrate the stand-out gardens and plants on display. They are joined by a team of green-fingered experts with advice for viewers to create a garden that helps nature to flourish. Toby Buckland shows how to create a border that wildlife will love, Flo Headlam explores a city garden that's a sensory sanctuary and Sue Kent has a guide to help beginner gardeners get growing

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: The designers travel to Leeds, where they must transform studio apartments, creating cleverly divided open-plan spaces to entice potential renters in Leeds. Alan follows their progress throughout, while Michelle Ogundehin is joined by former winner Lynsey Ford to critique the final results, before another hopeful is asked to pack up their design kit

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Knockout week begins with the first seven of the 14 remaining cooks given the chance to work in their first professional kitchen. But first, Anna Haugh and Grace Dent ask them to deliver a single dish worthy of being served in a restaurant, pop-up or even featured in a cookbook. For two, this will be the end of the road, while the others spend the day at the renowned Roe restaurant in Canary Wharf

programme.title: Half Man
programme.desc: The drama moves to 2008, and after a breakdown, Niall learns that Ruben has returned. Obsession takes hold, and a charged confrontation forces the brothers to face their fractured past. Explosive drama, written by and starring Richard Gadd, with Jamie Bell

programme.title: The Krays (1990)
programme.desc: Biopic of the infamous twin brothers who built up a criminal empire in the East End of London during the 1960s. Seizing territory from an already established rival by force, they quickly rose to prominence in the city's gangland culture. However, over time the brothers began to clash, while enemies stood ready to bring them down. Gangster biopic, starring Gary and Martin Kemp, Billie Whitelaw and Tom Bell.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show 2026
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman and Angellica Bell launch the BBC RHS People's Choice award, where viewers can vote for their favourite large show garden. The gardening experts are back with more inspiration from this year's show, including how to create impressive house plants displays, as well as revealing the secrets to growing some of the UK's favourite plants

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Briony May Williams helps a sporty couple who claim to have viewed over a thousand properties online in the search for their dream home in rural Suffolk

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road

programme.desc: Dominic Chinea and Will Kirk help Sussex Fire and Rescue Service repair their soapbox racer, Babie, and Kirsten Ramsay restores a collectable gnome. Dom tries his hand at glass blowing and a father-and-daughter duo blend science and art in their colourful neon creations

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Friends Craig, Kerry, Tony and Louise compete for cash by crossing a bridge of stepping stones across the studio floor. Quiz show, hosted by Ross Kemp

programme.title: Pointless

At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Susan Wokoma

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Angellica Bell and Alex Jones present the magazine show, with celebrity guests and stories of interest from around the UK

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The people of Walford gather for Nigel's funeral. Grant worries about Mark. The Mitchells head off on a mission

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Knockout week continues, and the second set of seven amateur cooks must prove themselves enough to make it into their first professional kitchen. First they must prove to judges Anna Haugh and Grace Dent they have what it takes by serving up a single dish worthy of being served in a restaurant, pop-up or even featured in a cookbook, with only the first best getting the chance to deliver a lunchtime service at the Straits Kitchen restaurant at London's Pan Pacific Hotel

programme.title: Amandaland
programme.desc: Amanda finds a used condom in a sofa she's disposing of and tries to work out which family member could have left it there. Comedy, starring Lucy Punch and Joanna Lumley

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: With Ken needing to resit his driving exam in order to win back his licence, Richard offers to take him out on a mock test, not realising just how rusty his dad has become behind the wheel

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of today's headlines

programme.title: Carl Frampton: On Probation
programme.desc: Carl visits a former probationer facing fresh allegations of assault, but the incident is said to have happened years before she was on probation. As the threat of jail looms over her, Carl explores why the case wasn't tried sooner, and asks what will happen to the life she has built if she is found guilty. He also meets a woman who ended up behind bars for her first offence and finds out how probation has helped her find her purpose again

programme.title: Saving Private Ryan (1998)
programme.desc: In the aftermath of the Normandy landings, a group of American soldiers scours the battlefields of France in search of a missing infantryman, who has been given compassionate leave to return home because his brothers have been killed in the conflict. Steven Spielberg's Oscar-winning Second World War drama, starring Tom Hanks, Tom Sizemore, Edward Burns, Matt Damon, Barry Pepper and Vin Diesel. It won five Academy Awards, including Best Cinematography, Best Film Editing, and Best Director. The film makers also took home a couple of Baftas for Best Sound and Best Special Effects

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: A panel of BBC journalists hosts round-up from Westminster, delivering the usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Small Town, Big Riot
programme.desc: Mobeen Azhar's investigation reveals a white supremacist that was campaigning outside the Suites hotel ahead of the protest may have influenced the riot. He also heads further afield to Scampton, Lincolnshire where residents have been campaigning against the Home Office's planned asylum centre in the former RAF base

programme.title: BBC New Comedy Awards
programme.desc: Alexandra Palace in north London, venue of the BBC's first ever television broadcast, stages the south of England heat, as comics Blank Peng, Fab Goualin, Sharifa Butterfly, Sydney May and Evaldas Karosas battle it out to book their place in this year's grand final showdown. Host Sara Pascoe is joined by stand-up comics Catherine Bohart and Michael Odewale, who appear alongside Fatiha El-Ghorri on the panel of judges

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Alex Murphy. Conor and Jock finally reunite after Jock makes a lucky escape from the Colombian prison and becomes a stowaway on a ship bound for Cork

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: The lads decide it's time to expand their empire in Cork city, as their days of doing all the hard work are over - they plan on being the bosses now. Starring Alex Murphy and Hilary Rose

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor's one true love Linda Walsh is getting married to another man, which can only lead to chaos at the wedding. Comedy, starring Alex Murphy

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall dives into the world of the caiman, learns about the plight of the world's biggest parrot and tries to find the giant river otter

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall heads to a place very few people ever go: Snake Island in Brazil, where the concentration of snakes is higher than anywhere else on earth

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: In the penultimate leg, the race shifts up a gear as the four remaining teams enter Mongolia, their eighth and final country. Facing freezing temperatures and rugged off-road travel, the intrepid duos set out on an expedition across the endless steppe and deep into the unknown, traversing landscapes few travellers have ever experienced. It tests every ounce of their resilience as they battle to take pole position at the final checkpoint before the finish line

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip and Ravi find out more about Andie's secret life. When Pip is presented with a new piece of evidence, she reaches a crisis point and questions whether Sal is guilty. Drama, starring Emma Myers (Wednesday)

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip faces dark consequences when she tries to confront the killer but just as all seems lost, the terrible truth about Andie Bell's disappearance is revealed

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip must come to terms with the repercussions of solving the case. However something doesn't seem quite right, and she places herself in danger once more to find out the whole truth. Detective drama, starring Emma Myers

programme.title: Small Town, Big Riot
programme.desc: Journalist Mobeen Azhar visits Kirkby, Merseyside to find out how a peaceful protest outside a hotel housing asylum seekers turned into a riot in February 2023 and uncovers the blueprint for a national wave of violent riots that took place across the country in the summer of 2024. Mobeen is soon confronted with serious allegations about what a group of asylum seekers have supposedly been up to in the small town, and attempts to uncover the truth but locals close ranks and warn each other against speaking to him, claiming Mobeen and the film crew will twist their words to make them look bad

programme.title: Confessions of a Teenage Fraudster
programme.desc: How a 16-year-old used his job in a call centre in Glasgow to go on a five-year crime wave, defrauding innocent cardholders and laying the groundwork for a life of wealth, first-class travel and an escape from the life he was destined for

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Part one of two. Tir Dhondy reveals the realities behind modern Las Vegas. The first episode explores the risk of crime faced by tourists and visits a pool party where massive screens show scores of live sports events from around the world so that partygoers can bet from the comfort of their sun lounger

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Part two of two. Tir Dhondy goes further into the underbelly of Las Vegas to investigate how the reinvention of the city to pull in a new generation of visitors is affecting locals

programme.title: Fake Friend: The Ticket Scammer
programme.desc: The case of a Somerset high-flyer who turned his charm and connections into tools of deception, conning his friends and hundreds of others, while living a life of glamour himself. Through the voices of his victims and event industry insiders, this documentary uncovers the high personal and financial cost of chasing exclusivity, and how easily even the most cautious can be deceived

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall heads into the heart of the Suriname rainforest in South America, searching for poison dart frogs, deadly snakes and the elusive giant river otter

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall goes in search of the deadliest cats in Africa in the carnivore capital of Zambia. He gets up close to the rulers of the Savannah - the lions and leopards

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: The designers travel to Leeds, where they must transform studio apartments, creating cleverly divided open-plan spaces to entice potential renters in Leeds. Alan follows their progress throughout, while Michelle Ogundehin is joined by former winner Lynsey Ford to critique the final results, before another hopeful is asked to pack up their design kit

programme.title: Green Book (2018)
programme.desc: In the 1960s, working-class Italian-American bouncer Tony Lip becomes the driver of Dr Donald Shirley, an African-American classical pianist on a tour of venues through the American South. Director Peter Farrelly's Bafta and Oscar-winning comedy drama, starring Viggo Mortensen, Mahershala Ali and Linda Cardellini

programme.title: Kidnapped by My Mum
programme.desc: The story of Alex Batty, who went missing when he was 11 on what was supposed to be a short family holiday with his mother and grandfather. Now aged 20, he tells the full story about his abduction and his life on the run, retracing the route of his disappearance across Spain and France, uncovering not just how he was hidden but the belief system that kept him there

programme.title: Confessions of a Teenage Fraudster
programme.desc: Between the ages of 17 and 21, Elliot Castro managed to commit fraudulent activity amounting to a estimated £2 million. This documentary continues his story. With a trail of crime stretching across several continents as he continued to live life on the run, then 18-year-old Elliot became more paranoid than ever and a stint in foreign jail seemed just around the corner

programme.title: Confessions of a Teenage Fraudster
programme.desc: After four years travelling the world spending fraudulently accrued money, it seems inevitable that Elliot will be deported into the hands of British police, who have put together an extensive case of his crimes. Once again though, he has other ideas. In truth, the one thing Elliot desires as much as freedom is a settled life. But even when he thinks he's found it, with a new home and friends, there is no escape from his trail of lies and deception. Eventually, justice seems set to catch up with him

programme.title: Stranger in My Family
programme.desc: Documentary following a man's search for his biological father in Portugal, enlisting the help of family friends and distant relations he finds online. Luke returns to the bar where his mother met the man in question 30 years previously, where a local historian and forensic genealogist helps him to find the answers he is looking for and understand his newfound heritage

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: In the penultimate leg, the race shifts up a gear as the four remaining teams enter Mongolia, their eighth and final country. Facing freezing temperatures and rugged off-road travel, the intrepid duos set out on an expedition across the endless steppe and deep into the unknown, traversing landscapes few travellers have ever experienced. It tests every ounce of their resilience as they battle to take pole position at the final checkpoint before the finish line

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Charlotte Smith and Adam Henson visit Oswestry Livestock Market, one of the region's largest weekly cattle and sheep markets. As spring trading peaks, they step into a fast-moving world where fortunes can change in minutes. With livestock numbers falling and markets closing nationwide, Oswestry remains a crucial lifeline, not just for trade, but for farming communities to come together

programme.title: Twenty Twenty Six
programme.desc: With the start of the tournament weeks away, final plans for the opening ceremony need to be pinned down. But its Mexican creative director has had a rethink, and his new vision doesn't include any reference to America at all, or indeed to soccer. The lead events producer may or may not be close to a having a breakdown. Also, Ian finds himself starting to think about life after football

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Denise Nurse helps a paralympic rower and his family return home to Carmarthenshire from Wokingham, and catches up with another family who made a similar move

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Roo Irvine is at the helm as the red and blue teams shop for antiques in Herefordshire, with assistance from Richard Madley and Charles Hanson. It all comes down to a battle of the bonus buys at the auction in Wrexham

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on an adventure through the western Mediterranean islands of Sardinia and Corsica, beginning in Sardinia's capital of Cagliari. He delves into the city's Roman past and experiences warm hospitality with a taste of the traditional myrtle liqueur, and discovers the legacy of Eleanora de Arborea, the pioneering medieval ruler who shaped Sardinia's laws

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.sub-title: One Clue, One Cow and a Soil-Based Breakthrough
programme.desc: A determined detective in Watford, Hertfordshire, begins painstakingly piecing together a pattern of farm equipment thefts that crosses county lines. As the investigation rapidly unfolds, his hunch leads to a major breakthrough and the dramatic capture of two men behind a £44,000 crime spree. In Leicestershire, a specialist fire and rescue team joins forces with a local farmer to rescue a heavily pregnant cow that has found itself trapped in a swimming pool. In Merthyr Mawr, Bridgend, reporter Paul Colgan meets the scientists pioneering an extraordinary new forensic technique that uses soil DNA to track illegal off-road bikers

programme.title: Lost and Found in the Lakes
programme.desc: The detectorists try to find a ring for a shepherd lost 60 years ago, and Mui takes on a phone found by an underwater drone. Her search for the owner goes international

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Four more contestants take to the black chair for the latest heat, with specialist subjects Neil Armstrong, Shakespeare's comedies, The Jacobite Rebellion of 1745, and Rupert Bear. Presented by Clive Myrie

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz in which contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions in the hopes of winning a £3,000 jackpot

programme.title: Clean It, Fix It
programme.desc: Single mother Hayley and daughter Rosie Bell live in a former council house with virtually no storage room. The team hopes to reclaim the empty wall space and find a way to upcycle a piece of furniture that gives mum and daughter a place to eat together

programme.title: Wonders of Life
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox travels across America encountering a diverse range of creatures that reveal how the senses evolved. In Kentucky, he explains how microscopic single-celled organism the paramecium can orientate itself through touch alone, and that catfish build a picture of their murky river environment through the taste buds that cover their bodies. Investigating the eyes of octopuses, Brian finds a link between the ability to process sensory data and intelligence, which may be evidence that humans evolved large brains in order to make sense of what they could see

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel and the experts value items of interest from Houghton Hall in Norfolk, including a costume worn by actor Yul Brynner, a poacher's gun in the guise of a walking stick and a collection of Oxo memorabilia. Among the more precious artefacts are a collection of 100 silver teething rattles, a small pottery cat and a rare Anglo-Saxon ring found in a garden

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin heads to historic places around the UK, including Capesthorne Hall in Cheshire, where Anita Manning has a surprise in store for one lucky woman. Paul also visits Chedworth Roman Villa in the Cotswolds

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Josh Jones, Molly Rainford, Mark Rhodes and Sonia test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's railway tour of Sardinia heads inland towards the island's mountain landscapes, which he discovers were frequented by DH Lawrence. He visits the Nuraghe, mysterious Bronze Age conical stone structures unique to Sardinia where artefacts have been unearthed to reveal an advanced society of engineers, navigators, and craftsmen

programme.title: Marcus Wareing Simply Provence
programme.desc: Marcus loves a British fruit based pud, and is searching Provence for the local equivalent. He's also looking for the tastiest local cheese to take to the village pétanque game

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show 2026
programme.desc: On Medals Day, emotions are running high as the all-important medals are handed out. Monty Don and Rachel de Thame showcase the stand-out gardens across the showground, while Carol Klein reveals what it takes to grow Gold-Medal winning plants in the Great Pavilion. Adam Frost has inspirational design ideas for viewers' gardens at home and Jamie Butterworth explores some of the new plants on display. Also, the winner of the RHS Chelsea Garden of the Year will be unveiled

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Host Sandi Toksvig is in search of victory with panellists Rob Beckett, Daliso Chaponda, Lara Ricote and Alan Davies

programme.title: If Walls Could Talk
programme.desc: An exploration of some of Northern Ireland's best-loved buildings through the words of local people who have spent time with their walls, beginning with St Columb's Hall in Londonderry. Friends, family and colleagues come together to tell the story of this former Temperance Hall in the heart of the city and how it has endured throughout a century of change

programme.title: Carl Frampton: On Probation
programme.desc: The former boxer explores the world of the probation service. In the first episode, he meets a woman whose time on probation is drawing to an end, but who is now facing jail time for assaulting police officers. He also talks to a man trying to get his life back on track following years of drug abuse and who credits probation, and one staff member in particular, with turning his life around

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Victoria Derbyshire

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Premier League matches, featuring Bournemouth v Manchester City at Vitality Stadium, Arsenal v Burnley at Emirates Stadium and Chelsea v Tottenham Hotspur at Stamford Bridge. City and Arsenal continued their bids to win the title, while Spurs looked for vital points in their battle to avoid relegation

programme.title: Later... with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Jools returns to Alexandra Palace Theatre in north London for the 68th run of the show. Former One Direction member Niall Horan performs a couple of songs, including the title track from his forthcoming album Dinner Party, while veteran pop band Squeeze also play and chat to Jools at the piano about how their new record Trixies came about. Also appearing are dance-pop duo Tomora, who are Norwegian singer-songwriter Aurora and Tom Rowlands of the Chemical Brothers, as well as American poet and activist Aja Monet and Bristol rock and pop duo Getdown Services

programme.title: Iolo's River Valleys
programme.desc: Iolo Williamsheads up the Tywi river valley. His 75-mile journey begins at the coastal village of Llansteffan, where the river meets the sea under the watchful gaze of its 12th-century Norman castle. Following the river inland through the fertile lowlands, Iolo visits Bishop's Pond, by Abergwili. At Dinefwr Park near Llandeilo, Iolo heads into the parkland during the autumn for the Fallow Deer rutting season

programme.title: This Is a Bomb: The Nevada Casino Heist
programme.desc: A look at how, meticulous and methodical, Big John engineered his ransom scheme with military precision. On paper, it was flawless, but as the deadline approached, nothing went according to plan. Two battles unfolded simultaneously - a high-stakes ransom exchange and a desperate race to stop a bomb designed to be unstoppable

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: Livestock farmer Joyce has a rare tumour in her bile duct and is undergoing a complex operation to have it removed. During the life-saving procedure, Anya Adair and Professor Steve Wigmore encounter a difficult dilemma with huge consequences. Also, surgeon Malcolm Will has taken on a rare case involving a tumour that has invaded his patient's chest wall

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: City-dwelling families, couples and individuals set out to find their ideal rural retreats with the help of a property expert

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The teams scour an antiques centre in Kingston upon Thames, aided by Jonathan Pratt and Kate Bliss. Auctioneer Raj Bisram tells Charlie Ross about his career as a ski instructor

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's railway tour of Sardinia heads inland towards the island's mountain landscapes, which he discovers were frequented by DH Lawrence. He visits the Nuraghe, mysterious Bronze Age conical stone structures unique to Sardinia where artefacts have been unearthed to reveal an advanced society of engineers, navigators, and craftsmen

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels to the cultural heart of Japan, Kyoto, the city that for a thousand years was the capital. At the Rozanji Temple, he learns about a book that is described as the world's first novel, the Tale of Genji. At Kyoto's Matsunoo shrine, Michael gets a beginner's guide to shrine etiquette and at the end of the day he takes a trip to a local restaurant to meet Satoha, an apprentice geisha

programme.title: Great Korean Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael's South Korean railway journey continues in Seoul. Travelling across one of the world's largest metro systems, he heads south to Suwon to visit Samsung's headquarters. Touring a futuristic smart home, Michael explores how innovation now shapes everyday life

programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Four more contestants take to the black chair, with specialist subjects The Peter Wimsey Stories of Dorothy L Sayers, Arnold Schwarzenegger, The Wire and The Falklands Conflict. Presented by Clive Myrie

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz in which contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions in the hopes of winning a £3,000 jackpot

programme.title: Clean It, Fix It
programme.desc: The team comes to the aid of a family of four who are bursting at the seams in their two-bedroom flat in south-west London, with the hallway a particular pinch-point

programme.title: Wonders of Life
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox travels to southern Africa and Madagascar to examine why Earth is such a fertile place and how it sustains such a large diversity of life forms. He searches for clues by considering the chemistry of a lion and reveals that the answer lies partly in carbon, a universal building block forged by the stars that is captured and used by the living world. By following the stellar origins of the element and the universality of the laws of nature, Brian contemplates whether there could be similar life elsewhere in the cosmos

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel presents from Harrogate in North Yorkshire, where the experts examine several Navy log books belonging to a captain who had connections with Napoleon. Plus, a silk bedcover and a carriage clock made for the daughter of a great Victorian philanthropist

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Members of the public try to make money out of their antiques. Presented by Paul Martin, with Nick Davies and Elizabeth Talbot

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Josh Jones, Molly Rainford, Mark Rhodes and Sonia test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his Sardinian railway adventure, arriving into Mamoiada to investigate the town's pagan traditions. In a mask, black sheepskin and cowbells, he embraces the role of a mamuthone, who wards off evil spirits. Taking a 1950s diesel railcar, Michael heads to Macomer, where he discovers the railway's surprising connection to a pioneering Welsh railway engineer

programme.title: Iolo's River Valleys
programme.desc: Iolo Williams explores the Teifi, known as the queen of Welsh rivers. He heads upstream to the Teifi marshes and the old slate quarries near Cilgerran, where he finds a slow worm hunting in the open. He also meets Rod Bowen, a local coracleman, to learn about a 2,000-year-old fishing tradition. A major highlight occurs at Maes y Crugiau, where Iolo watches an otter as it hunts and eats an eel

programme.title: The Future with Hannah Fry
programme.sub-title: OK Computer, How Do I Feel?
programme.desc: Hannah Fry explores whether artificial intelligence read emotions better than a human, as well as the new technology creeping into people's lives

programme.title: The Secret Genius of Modern Life
programme.desc: Hannah Fry uncovers and literally un-packs the secrets behind the technologies of the modern world in a series of short clips

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show 2026
programme.desc: Monty Don and Arit Anderson explore how gardening can inspire people to be more creative, while celebrating the artistry on display in the show ground and Great Pavilion. Rachel de Thame will be revealing who has been named RHS Florist of the Year 2026 and Adam Frost will be delving into the latest design trends and explaining how viewers can replicate them at home

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: A surgeon must remove part of his patient's tongue and reconstruct it to save her from an aggressive cancer, and surgeons perform a rare simultaneous pancreas and kidney transplant

programme.title: Peelers: The PSNI for Real
programme.desc: Stephen Nolan joins officers from the Police Service of Northern Ireland as they patrol the streets of Belfast. Response officers Anna and Perri race to the aid of a man who has had bleach squirted directly on his face and into his eyes. Across town, Tom and Nigel race to the scene of a serious crash that has a left a pedestrian badly injured

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Victoria Derbyshire

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Unspun World with John Simpson
programme.desc: The BBC's world affairs editor reflects on the week's major global news stories, talking to local experts from around the world to go behind the headlines

programme.title: The Turning (2020)
programme.desc: A woman quits her teaching job to be a governess for a young heiress who witnessed her parent's tragic deaths, and ends up also teaching the girl's problematic brother after he is sent home from boarding school. The tutor has some strange, unexplainable experiences in the house and begins to suspect there is more to their story than meets the eye. Modern adaptation of Henry James's gothic horror 1898 novella The Turn of the Screw, starring Mackenzie Davis, Finn Wolfhard, Brooklynn Prince and Joely Richardson

programme.title: This Is a Bomb: The Nevada Casino Heist
programme.desc: When the bomb detonated inside Harvey's Wagon Wheel Casino, it left behind more than shattered concrete and twisted steel in the form of a maze of unanswered questions. However, a lucky break in the case led to a psychological stand-off where calculated risks unravelled, loyalties fractured, and the truth proved far more complicated than anyone imagined

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The last heat of this year's competition has arrived, and the final group of six amateurs have been waiting for their shot at culinary stardom. Anna Haugh and Grace Dent will be putting them through their paces across two tasks. The hopefuls include two teachers, a pub manager and a civil servant. The signature dish round kicks off proceedings, with Anna and Grace hoping to sample a creation that represents the amateurs down to a tee

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Marianna Spring explores how AI-generated videos are shaping what people see, what they believe and who they can trust. From scam adverts and cloned voices to deepfake fraud, political manipulation and racially charged disinformation, the programme reveals how cheap and easily available AI tools are being weaponised

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Grace Dent and Anna Haugh present the last round of heats, with six more amateur chefs serving up a dinner party menu that will have guests begging for the recipe. The dishes in this round include a posh cheese sandwich and a middle eastern dessert. With the two best efforts granted a place in the quarter finals, the remaining four must prove their worth with a meringue roulade

programme.title: The Future with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: Hannah travels from California to Japan to experience the latest age-defying techniques and examine the true meaning of life

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don makes a start on planting up some of his spectacular summer containers. He's also got leeks to plant out and shares some advice on how to make your own fertiliser. Frances Tophill heads to Berkshire to learn more about biodynamic gardening and the benefits it can bring. It's also time for an update from Adam Frost's new garden, where things are slowly starting to take shape

programme.desc: A contemplation over whether Earth could have been visited by extra-terrestrials in its ancient past. New discoveries suggest contact may have influenced human art and technology

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Modern Day Dinosaur Sighting, Mysterious Lights Above Norway, and Girl Punches Down Tree
programme.desc: An examination of several seemingly impossible things 'caught on film'. This episodes features reports of a dinosaur living in the African Congo, strange lights spotted above the Hessdalen Valley in Norway, a 12-year-old girl in Russia with superhuman strength and proof of an alien structure on an asteroid

programme.title: Junk & Disorderly: Henry's Wreck-trospective
programme.desc: Compilation of episodes from the show Junk & Disorderly, which features Henry Cole and other restorers buying, fixing, and selling automotive junk

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: A customer is furious when Seth rumbles him for trying to flog fake goods. Later, panic grips the staff as a farmer lets an animal loose in the shop

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Les is furious when he learns that dealers are poaching his customers, and things get worse when he tells a client that a diamond watch is fake

programme.desc: Highlights from the show, as David Dickinson and his team of antiques dealers offer members of the public the chance to take their items to auction or accept a cash payment. programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team visit Bolton, Greater Manchester, where a Hermes travel clock, some Bolton Wanderers memorabilia and a tiger's eye parasol impress antique dealers Jo Brayshaw and Simon Schneider

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: The team tries to make a deal on a guitar signed by Crosby, Stills & Nash, and also examines a pair of bomb fins from the Second World War

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: The guys value an air mail letter delivered by aviation pioneer Charles Lindbergh on the Spirit of St Louis, as well as a collection of mini bikes and a baseball bat from the 1900s

programme.desc: Bogart tries to get his father in shape in time for his wedding. Meanwhile, Roy brings a mime to an auction in Corktown

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.desc: The buyers have high hopes for a container auction in a town named Utopia, but it remains to be seen whether they will uncover any household treasures worth celebrating

programme.desc: Paul's brother Barry must make sure his standards are high when he is asked to fill in for Paul and Bogart at an auction in Brampton

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.desc: Roy decides to bring his young nephew and protege to experience the highs and lows of an auction in South Etobicoke

programme.title: Storage Wars: Texas

programme.desc: Mary shows a different side to her personality in Dallas and soon has a roaring crowd on its feet. Meanwhile, Ricky and Bubba are led astray by their latest find

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Miquita and Andi Oliver, Mo Gilligan with Babatunde Aléshé, Jamie Carragher and Micah Richards, Nick Grimshaw and his niece Liv, Big Zuu, Hyder and Tubsey, Shaun Ryder and Bez, Clare Balding and Alice Arnold, and Denise van Outen and Duncan James comment on the week's TV. Programmes discussed include Glastonbury, Hijack, A Wright Family Holiday, Cocaine Bear, Below Deck Sailing Yacht, Puzzling and Antiques Roadshow. First shown in June 2023

programme.title: Love Cheats
programme.desc: The case of a woman who claimed to have MS and conned her partner into running up huge debts to fund treatment, before falsely accusing him of domestic violence. When she threatened to kill herself in Japan, unless friends raise $20,000 that she claimed had been stolen, people started to question the truth of her stories and the FBI became involved

programme.title: Salisbury Poisonings: The Untold Story
programme.desc: The documentary picks up four months after the attempted assassination, when there was still no sign of the source of the Novichok. Salisbury re-opened after months under lockdown - only for civilians Dawn Sturgess and Charlie Rowley to fall ill in nearby Amesbury. As the death of the former turned it into a murder investigation, questions began to be asked about how well the UK reacted to such unprecedented Russian aggression and the stark security lessons that were learnt

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef again comes to the aid of failing American restaurants, starting in Fishtown, Philadelphia, where he tries to help the three owners of the Hot Potato Cafe, which has not made a profit in two years. Spurred on by a scathing review by an influential critic, he makes radical changes to alter the staff's attitudes and create food that is up to his own exacting standards

programme.title: Extraordinary Escapes with Sandi Toksvig
programme.desc: Sandi and sketch comedian Doon Mackichan visit Donegal in the Republic of Ireland to see what it has to offer. Their first stop is at Tuath na Mara which boasts access to a secluded sandy cove, perfect for outdoor swimming enthusiast Doon. Sandi shares a personal confession and the pair go for an icy dip in the North Atlantic. Next, the duo stay at the eco-resort Lough Mardal Lodge where they meet miniature cows and share stories of Mongolian princesses. They end their adventure in the countryside just north of Donegal Bay where they stay in Candlewick Cottage in which Doon serenades Sandi with improvised comedy tunes on the piano

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Country Lives
programme.desc: Dave and Lynn need help with the layout and decoration of their living room, while Kay plans her first ever craft fair

programme.title: Hollyoaks Omnibus
programme.desc: Frankie wants to come clean to Caleb, while Charlie's crumbling under the weight of his lies, and the village gathers for Rex and Ste's wedding. Diane reflects on her life in Hollyoaks as the Osbornes and Keanes feud downstairs in The Dog. Wanting to make every moment count, the Hutchinsons leave the customers to it and join Diane

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Lucy Porter in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against words and numbers

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam and Diane go to dinner with Frasier and his new live-in girlfriend Lilith, but the evening proves far from successful. Comedy, starring Ted Danson and Bebe Neuwirth

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: A friend of Frasier's drops in for a drink at the bar and when Diane learns he is an eminent marriage guidance counsellor, she asks him to assess her relationship with Sam. John Cleese guest stars

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam asks small-town boy Woody to chaperone his god-daughter Joyce round town now she has moved to Boston - but soon sees the disadvantages of his choice. Ted Danson and Woody Harrelson star

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Robert is ecstatic when he is recommended for a job with the FBI, until it starts to look like Marie's intrusive ways may scupper his chances

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray and Debra amuse the guests at a party by doing a wickedly accurate impression of Frank and Marie - who are not amused and respond in kind with an impersonation of their own

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra finds a cassette that contains a message from Ray's college girlfriend, and wants to know why he has saved it for all these years. She also shows Ray some of her keepsakes from previous relationships, prompting a severe case of jealousy on his part

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The shrink falls head over heels for an policewoman, unaware she finds his dad a much more interesting proposition. Comedy, guest starring Malcolm in the Middle's Jane Kaczmarek

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The radio station's new owner is about to visit, so Frasier decides to do a little grovelling - especially as the man could make his show a nationwide success

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Roz begins to date a compulsive womaniser who also happens to be one of Niles' patients, but when Frasier tries to warn her she is heading for heartache, she mistakes his concern for jealousy. Comedy, starring Peri Gilpin and Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The shrink's radio show survives a stern focus-group test, but he cannot help wondering why one of the members was less impressed than the others. Meanwhile, Daphne finds the thought of returning to Manchester difficult. Comedy, guest starring Tony Shalhoub (Monk), with Kelsey Grammer and Jane Leeves

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to Eagle Rock in California to try to change the fortunes of Capri, a grubby restaurant that often remains closed during lunchtime because of the twin owners' lazy attitude. A crisis ensues as soon as Gordon instructs the duo to clean the place, revealing that only tough measures are likely to save the business

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In Somerset, Simon O'Brien and Henry Cole meet sisters Katherine and Bex, who run an animal sanctuary and a theatre props business. Restorations and upcycles include work on a giant guinea pig hutch

programme.title: Help! We Bought a Village
programme.desc: The owners of a hamlet in France visit two mysterious stone towers on the edge of their land. A couple from Manchester host the descendants of a farmer who lived in their village

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the long-running words-and-numbers game with Lucy Porter joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Helen from Buckinghamshire retired recently and is now looking for her dream holiday home for herself and her two children in La Zenia, south-western Spain. Helping Helen with this house-hunt is her best friend Shelley, who plans to come out and stay. For her budget of £105,000 Helen wants Craig Rowe to assist in finding her a three-bedroom house or apartment with a communal pool

programme.title: Escape to Florida
programme.desc: Seán and Áine's Apollo Beach dream has been severely tested by a recent hurricane. Laura juggles romance and horse dentistry during Independence Day celebrations in Groveland, and film director Damian braves alligators in the Everglades

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: In the Charente, Sophie and Ray struggle to bring the modern to the ancient. At Château Domaine de la Barde, a laundry room renovation has Krys and Tim in a spin. And at Château de Cézérac, Jon and Carmen encounter problems with their potager

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Alicante, Charlotte Rodriguez and her brother-in-law Damon are looking for a new holiday home for insurance broker Darren and his wife Tanya from Essex. On the Costa del Sol, husband-and-wife team Andrew and Shani Hamilton meet up with repeat clients David, wife Jackie and son Ryan, who are looking for an upgraded holiday home

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Jamie's Ultimate BBQ
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver cooks his barbecue breakfast, which takes a much-loved classic to smoky heights, then serves up his smoke-kissed sesame chicken burger, accompanied by crunchy slaw and Korean-inspired sauce. His veggie barbecue platter offers the perfect spread, comprising smoky baba ghanoush, dressed roasted peppers, minty courgettes and halloumi cheese with apricots, then finishes off with his surf 'n' turf mixed grill, cooked on a plancha griddle with butter and wine

programme.title: Falling
programme.desc: Drama written by Jack Thorne, and starring Keeley Hawes and Paapa Essiedu. Sister Anna has been living a peaceful life as a nun in a convent for more than two decades. But a chance encounter with intriguing priest Father David leaves her with an uneasy feeling and threatens to disrupt the order that has governed her whole life so far.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Staff in Major Trauma at Queen's Medical Centre in Nottingham are overstretched and pushed to the limit as they face ever-increasing patient numbers. Jeremy and Patricia are admitted with multiple injuries following a high-speed car crash. Doctors are concerned that Jeremy's history of cancer could affect his recovery, while the emergency team tries to determine the full extent of Patricia's spinal injury. Andrew, 45, is in resus after falling three metres from a ladder at work and seriously fracturing his ankle. Medics need to re-align his bones before emergency surgery

programme.title: Marilyn and the Mob
programme.desc: Part two of two. In the summer of 1962, Marilyn travelled to Nevada with Frank Sinatra, placing her among mob figures and dangerous rumours. Days later, she was found dead in her Los Angeles home. Although her death was ruled a suicide, circumstances and later investigations fuelled decades of speculation. Marilyn was caught between fame, political power and organised crime. But was her death a conspiracy or an accident?

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: The crew of Warwickshire, Northamptonshire, Derbyshire, Leicestershire and Rutland Air Ambulance race to reach Anthony, who's in traumatic cardiac arrest after coming off his motorbike at high speed. In Buckingham, Thames Valley Air Ambulance look after Carol after she's been trampled by a foal and in Eccles-on-Sea, Carter needs the help of the East Anglian Air Ambulance service after coming off his jet ski. In Brundall, teenager Harry has an open leg fracture after walking in front of a car

programme.title: Wonders of the World I Can't See
programme.desc: Chris McCausland and Liza Tarbuck head to Canada to experience the majestic natural marvel that is Niagara Falls. It's the only wonder in the series that makes a noise, so Chris is especially excited to be here. The pair explore the wider Canadian cultural landscape of axe throwing, bushcraft and poutine before heading into the heart of the falls for an emotional final experience

programme.title: Extraordinary Escapes with Sandi Toksvig
programme.desc: Sandi is joined by comedian and author Rosie Jones for a journey round the Midlands exploring the best staycation retreats they have to offer. The pair begin in Rutland where they stay at the Rocket Lodge, an eco-build made of upcycled grain silos that boasts a wood-fired hot-tub, a circular kitchen and a disco bathroom. In the sand dunes on a strip of the Lincolnshire coastline, the duo stay in a rebooted 70s beach house called Sandy Feet where Sandi learns that 'Netflix and chill' doesn't mean what she thinks it does. They end their trip close to the Welsh border on the Walcot Estate in a 200-year-old hideaway known as the Wheelwright's shop that once lived 20 miles away but was dismantled and transported piece by piece before being rebuilt to look exactly the same

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to Flamangos restaurant in Whitehouse, New Jersey, where he tries to help the mother-and-daughter owners turn around their struggling business's fortunes. He faces a struggle because parent Adelle has a bad relationship with her staff and is disappointed by the resident chef, leaving Gordon with the added challenge of changing her attitude

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples from Blackburn compete to put on the best dinner party, with a halibut starter served in tinfoil, an eager-to-please pair of hosts, and a relaxed curry dinner

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Country Lives
programme.desc: James and Jodie find out how to make their 17th-century farmhouse watertight while Emma and James take two alpacas paddling at the beach

programme.title: Sunday Brunch
programme.desc: Highlights of the food and chat show, with Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the long-running words-and-numbers game with Lucy Porter joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Norm gets a new job with a prestigious Boston accountancy firm, but the position is not quite as glamorous as he leads people to believe. Meanwhile, Cliff finds some money

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: The gang makes a home movie chronicling an average day at Cheers in a bid to reassure a nervous Mr and Mrs Boyd back in Indiana that Woody has wonderful friends in Boston. Comedy, guest starring Doris Grau and Al Rosen

programme.title: Cheers
programme.sub-title: A House Is Not a Home
programme.desc: Sam and Diane buy the house they've always dreamed of, but their happiness is cut short when they begin to feel sorry for the previous owners. Comedy, starring Ted Danson and Shelley Long

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray steels himself for the delicate matter of telling Ally where babies come from - only to learn she is more interested in discussing the meaning of life itself. Ray Romano stars

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra runs for school president, but Ray decides to vote against her to avoid looking after the twins during all the meetings she would have to attend. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra tries to create a sense of affection and familiarity by addressing Marie as 'mom' - but the gesture backfires badly and offends her real mother, who finds it inappropriate

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Daphne tries to avoid returning to England to visit her family, while Frasier is given a recording of his first radio broadcast in Seattle. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and Jane Leeves

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Daphne's ex-fiance Clive visits Seattle to declare his undying love and ask her to marry him. Rather than saying she cannot see a future for them, she decides to let him down gently and pretends to be Niles's wife. With David Hyde Pierce and Jane Leeves

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Roz talks Frasier into going on a blind date with one of her friends - but Bulldog ends up falling in love with her. Meanwhile, a spelling mistake in an advert causes trouble for Niles, and Daphne accidentally destroys Martin's favourite shoes. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and Dan Butler

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: A recurring dream makes Frasier wonder whether he is gay, prompting him to search for an explanation of its meaning - with a little help from Niles. Comedy, with Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: In this revisit episode, chef Gordon Ramsay goes back to Mojito, the Junction (formerly known as Flamangos) and Bazzini to check in with the owners he helped in the past and find out whether his advice has been put to good use. Will he be pleased with their progress?

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Gemma Longworth are in Northern Ireland to meet Dave, whose workshop turns out to be a treasure trove including a church pew and a ladder that becomes a wardrobe

programme.title: Help! We Bought a Village
programme.desc: The owners of a village in the Limousin region of France discover their roof needs repairing. A Manchester couple host a wedding reception in their hamlet

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the long-running words-and-numbers game with Lucy Porter joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Mark, from Kent, has a lifelong love of Italy. Having recently retired, he's set his sights on finding a holiday bolthole in its Puglia region to escape to at various times of the year. With best friend John also on hand to lend his support, Mark would love to find a three-bedroom townhouse. Property expert Laura Hamilton also helps him deliver his dream on an £80,000 budget

programme.title: Escape to Florida
programme.desc: Seán gets into deep water with his Apollo Beach fishing charter sideline. Charmaine runs a multimillion-pound business from her Miami bedroom while homeschooling her daughter, and outside Orlando, Gary and Maria are expecting more grandchildren

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: Half-built walls and a ticking clock haunt Sophie and Ray at Château des Fouilloux. A massive water leak forces Stephanie into a full kitchen rebuild at Château de Lalande. And in Occitania, happily ever after seems a long way off for Janet and Philip

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: Sharon and Shaun Garner channel a higher power as they hope to lead reiki master Cathy to her ideal property in Almería. Former angler Kevin and his wife Lynne are keen to net a retirement home in Málaga with the help of Shani and Andrew Hamilton. Away from the office, Sharon and Shaun take part in a five-kilometre charity dog walk

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer search for properties in Wales, with one couple who crossed the world to live there and another who have always called it home. Tish and Ramone moved to the country from Cape Town and now they consider it their adopted home. It's Kirstie's job to find them a dream place that's both full of period charm and low maintenance. Phil is with Gemma and Ellis who are Welsh born and bred, but even with their local knowledge they are finding it difficult to get a future family home in Newport

programme.title: Falling
programme.desc: After her bold declaration, Anna attempts to adjust to the new path she has chosen but comes face to face with those from her past who disapprove. Meanwhile, as gossip spreads throughout the community, David tries to ignore the noise. But he clashes with the bishop and demons from his past threaten to rear their heads. Drama, starring Keeley Hawes and Paapa Essiedu

programme.title: Marilyn and the Mob
programme.desc: First in a two-documentary tracing Marilyn Monroe's rise from a troubled childhood to Hollywood superstardom, and focusing on how the entertainment industry she idolised was deeply entwined with organised crime. As Marilyn's star rose, she became involved with Hollywood figures tied to the underworld, including Frank Sinatra, Johnny Roselli, and Sam Giancana. Her romance with the debonair and politically connected star Peter Lawford brought her into the orbit of John F Kennedy and Robert F Kennedy, where Hollywood, crime and politics merged

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their thoughts on Prisoner, David Attenborough's 100 Years on Planet Earth, Countdown, First Dates, Scam Interceptors and news reports about the local elections and Keir Starmer

programme.title: In the Footsteps of Killers
programme.desc: Emilia Fox and Professor David Wilson investigate the 1993 murder of 42-year-old Bulic Forsythe, a manager at Lambeth Council, who was savagely beaten in his own home in Clapham, London, before the property was set ablaze

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay gives his advice to Paul Bazzini, an award-winning chef from New York who now owns a restaurant in Ridgewood, New Jersey. Paul confesses he has lost his passion for food and is struggling to keep up with the business, a situation that seems evident to other members of staff, his wife and customers

programme.title: The Last Tree (2019)
programme.desc: A British boy of Nigerian heritage enjoys a happy childhood with his foster mother in rural Lincolnshire, but his life is thrown into upheaval when he is returned to his biological mother, and uprooted to the unfamiliar world of inner city London. Drama, starring Samuel Adewunmi and Gbemisola Ikumelo

programme.title: Unreported World
programme.desc: Sahar Zand reports from the Philippines on the controversial world of spiritual healers. Across the country, with high medical costs and difficulties accessing healthcare, patients put their trust in alternative treatments to cure anything from chronic pain to cancer. Faith healers, blending Catholic rituals with centuries-old traditions, promise miraculous recoveries through prayer, herbal remedies and psychic surgery. Their clinics attract patients from across this country of 112 million people, and even overseas. Sahar meets families placing their hopes in these treatments, including those who've turned away from conventional medicine. Some say they've witnessed extraordinary recoveries, crediting healers with saving their lives. On the outskirts of Manilla, three faith healers who claim to treat over 100 people per week believe they've been divinely chosen to cure conditions modern medicine can't detect. But critics warn that the industry is dangerously unregulated. Medical professionals fear that patients are being misled, delaying vital treatment, often fatally. She hears from people who believe that their loved ones paid the ultimate price for putting their trust in miracle cures that they now believe were a scam. With faith healers operating across the country, authorities face mounting pressure to act, but regulation remains limited in a country where faith runs deep

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect visits a futuristic home designed to save space, with a double bed rising into the ceiling to reveal the living area beneath, and the kitchen and bathroom cleverly hidden from view. Inspired by these ideas, George and William make progress with their tree house, showing that by folding everything into the walls it is possible to fit all the rooms into a small space. He also meets a couple who are building the ultimate wilderness beach hut

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Country Lives
programme.desc: Jodie wants to start a small horse livery business and also explores how to start up a kids' club on her family's smallholding. Sarah Beeny follows her progress

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray and Rachel Riley host the long-running words-and-numbers game with Lucy Porter joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam and Diane finalise their wedding plans, but regulars at the bar still have reservations about the proposed nuptials. As the couple prepare to exchange their vows, Professor Sumner Sloan shows up to reveal a publisher is interested in one of Diane's manuscripts - if she is willing to leave with him immediately to start rewrites

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam's round-the-world sailing jaunt is cut short by a sunken ship, forcing him to return to the bar. However, his chances of getting his old job back are soon ruined by new manager Rebecca. Kirstie Alley makes her debut in the sitcom

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam agrees to fill in for a local sports broadcaster, thinking the job will be a breeze, but finds out the hard way it is not as easy as it looks. Comedy, starring Ted Danson

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Part one of three. Marie gives Debra the silent treatment for being rude to her, so Ray talks the pair into making amends - but as usual in the Barone household, his efforts backfire. Starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Part two of three. Marie and Debra's feud continues, so Ray and Frank use it to their advantage, while Robert tries to get the warring pair to make friends. American comedy, starring Ray Romano, Patricia Heaton and Doris Roberts

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: The crew of Warwickshire, Northamptonshire, Derbyshire, Leicestershire and Rutland Air Ambulance race to reach Anthony, who's in traumatic cardiac arrest after coming off his motorbike at high speed.
