programme.desc: A baby has a dangerously high temperature and the paramedics suspect sepsis, and a woman who is pregnant and has tested positive for Covid-19 has had a migraine for seven days. A woman has a blood pressure reading that's barely detectable. She is so ill, the paramedics are concerned that she may not survive a trip to hospital

programme.title: Our Tiny Islands
programme.desc: On Iona, sheep farmer Joanne cares for the family's flock, but also plays an integral role in wool production on the island, resulting in a product that's unique to the island. Meanwhile, 350 miles away on Lundy Island, we meet employees of the Lundy Company who are able to live on the island by virtue of their jobs there. On North Uist, Alexander is using his skills acquired as a gardener on the mainland to reinvigorate his nine-and-a-half-acre croft

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Rich, 35, is airlifted to the hospital after being involved in a high-speed collision on the M25, and doctors are concerned when they discover he has lost feeling below his chest and can't move his legs. Meanwhile, 36-year-old Justin has had a life-threatening accident while playing football and he is put into an induced coma while a CT scan is carried out, which reveals a potentially life-threatening bleed on his brain

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: This edition focuses on a mother's role in different family units. Jacob, 20, arrives in resus with suspected internal bleeding after crashing his car into a wall at high speed, while Joan, 82, is treated for a head wound after tripping down the stairs at home, and seven-year-old Jackson has a suspected infection on his cheek. Documentary following life inside the A&E department of St George's in south-west London, focusing on patients, their families and the staff dealing with the emergencies

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty and Matt Tebbutt embark on an extraordinary road trip through America's Deep South. Starting on the Gulf Coast, in New Orleans, they discover how the shells from the huge number of oysters consumed locally could protect the coastline. Heading through Mississippi, Jimmy and Matt find out what makes southern sweet tea different from a British cuppa. In Alabama, Matt wants to know why they fry green tomatoes, and Jimmy uncovers exactly what grits are. Then it's off to Tennessee to visit the Jack Daniels distillery and discover the secrets of sour mash. In Louisville, where the South meets the North, Jimmy and Matt learn about fast-food consistency at Kentucky Fried Chicken HQ

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Northamptonshire in search of more upcycling prospects. Simon finds some night vision goggles and an antique snooker table that's seen better days, while Henry chooses an enormous US army truck as one of his picks

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien search for more items to fix up and sell on, visiting a Norman castle, restoring a Fiat 500 and making a table from part of Southend pier

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition starts at Chimneys B&B in Holbrook in Suffolk, where owner Carolyn Spilling, who's joined by her friend Delia Duell, hopes that her uptempo personality will be a hit with the guests. However, guests Andrea and John are unimpressed to discover that they're in self-catering. At breakfast, Italian cousins Massimo and Raf aren't keen on the home from home approach

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to Neale and Julie Taylor's vegetarian B&B, Copperfields Guest House in Broadstairs, Kent. Carolyn and Delia give their room a thorough going over, while parking causes concerns for Andrea and John. The guests get competitive when they make bird boxes in the afternoon and at dinner, Neale and Julie make a revelation about a secret breakfast dish that intrigues chef Massimo

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at The George and Abbotsford in Melrose, Scotland, where Andria Gardner and John Marr aim to pull out all the stops to impress their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests spend the night at Massimo Perrulli's hotel in County Durham, where one couple have cleanliness concerns, another are missing a bath and the third have an axe to grind

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet to find out what they have been paid and settle some scores. Chimneys B&B's Carolyn and her friend Delia want some home truths from Massimo and Raf

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Sports fanatic James Cox hosts the first of the week's dinner parties in Northampton, and he is confident that his French-Italian menu will bag him the £1,000 prize. However, some of his guests - Barb Wiles, Donna Harris, Duncan Fielding and Caroline Nash - are left confused by his Vichyssoise soup starter, and the tiramisu causes one of them to choke

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Mother-of-three Donna Harris hosts the second dinner party in Northampton, and hopes that three courses of pure comfort-food will be enough to secure the £1,000 prize. Donna spends almost as much time fixing her make-up as she does preparing the meal, and when guests Duncan Fielding and Caroline Nash discover a secret stash of football shirts, they realise there is more to their hostess than meets the eye

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Part-time actress Caroline Nash is the host on day three of the competition in Northampton, and she hopes that a menu based on her travels will dazzle her guests. However, the night gets off to a shaky start as Donna Harris finds a hair on the side of her goat's cheese salad, while businessman Duncan Fielding seems more interested in goats themselves than the food

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Party-loving Barb Wiles hosts the fourth dinner party in the Northampton area, and hopes her Australian-themed menu will wow her guests and win her the £1,000 prize. However, the competitors are unnerved by her interest in extraterrestrials while snooping around her bedroom, and things go from bad to worse when a seafood starter and undercooked chicken result in tears at the dinner table

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Attention-loving businessman and keen cricketer Duncan Fielding takes centre stage in hosting the final dinner party from Northampton. He creates a menu based on the story of his life - which is full of surprises and full of spice - but it proves a challenge for three of his guests, who like their food mild. His main feature is a chicken and reindeer curry, and the table is impressed to discover that he runs a community pub from the bottom of his garden

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at the White Horse Inn in Maldon, Essex, where owners Jayne and Pete Kocinoga hope their attention to detail will win over their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to Barnfield B&B in St Osyth, Essex, run by Christine and Paul, where as well as offering rooms, Christine also runs spiritual retreats

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: For the third visit of the week the contestants head to The Black Swan Inn in Pickering, North Yorkshire, where husband and wife Phil and Jill Hall hope to impress their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stay is at Delamere Farm near Peterborough, where proud owner Agi Chapman is sent into a spin after hearing some critical comments over breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day arrives, and the hosts of The White Horse Inn, Barnfield B&B, The Black Swan Inn and Delamere Farm all discover just how much their guests valued their services

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Phil Spencer catches up with two sets of discerning house-hunters, each with very strong ideas on what they wanted. The searches may have been tough - in some instances they didn't even make it inside some of the properties - but both have faced even more challenging times since. In 2012, couple Robin and Colin wanted to move outside of London, but only an architectural gem was going to do. In 2014, Lia and Andrew said they were done renting in London's popular Isle of Dogs, and wanted to buy their own home now they were starting a family

programme.title: PopMaster TV
programme.desc: Ken Bruce hosts Britain's toughest music quiz, as five more players compete in the second heat, in six rounds, plus Original PopMaster and Three in Ten. The players with the highest scores across the series will secure a place in the Grand Final

programme.title: Ancient Greece by Train with Alice Roberts
programme.desc: The historian takes to the railways of Greece and Turkey as she learns about the Ancient Greek civilisation. Her journey begins in the northern Greek city of Thessaloniki, home of Alexander the Great, and visits a museum featuring a burial chamber for ancient Greek royalty. She heads south to Delphi to see the magnificent ruins of what is possibly the greatest ancient site in the world

programme.title: Castle Secrets
programme.sub-title: Nazi Crypt, the Cannibal's Caves, Hall of Screams
programme.desc: Delving into the stories behind historical fortifications. This edition explores the depths of a ruined castle where Hitler's dark occult dealings are revealed. Plus, learning about a Scottish clifftop bastion with a long history of criminal activity and a palace, which acted as a stage for betrayals

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Three boys are admitted for very different reasons, including cyclist Ollie, who arrives with head, neck and facial injuries having been involved in a traffic accident. Cameras also follow Dominic, another 16 year old. He was recovering at home after having his tonsils out when his throat started bleeding and he had to go to hospital

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Samuel, 24, is rushed to A&E after crashing his motorbike and being thrown into the road, while two-year-old Florence is brought in by her mum and dad, who suspect that she has swallowed a coin. Seventy-year-old Clive arrives after struggling to breathe at home, but his wife Linda has a difficult time keeping him in check, as he likes to tell a joke or two. Documentary following life inside the A&E department of St George's in south-west London, focusing on patients, their families and the staff

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Twenty-five-year-old Billy arrives at St George's by helicopter after being involved in a motorcycle accident in which a van ran over his chest. The team work quickly to get him into CT to see if he has any life-changing injuries, while his girlfriend, mum and stepdad desperately try to get to the hospital. Meanwhile, 58-year-old Stephen comes into A&E with his wife and son after showing signs of a stroke, and Milly brings her two-year-old son Freddie in after he jammed his thumb in a door

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A baby has a dangerously high temperature and the paramedics suspect sepsis, and a woman who is pregnant and has tested positive for Covid-19 has had a migraine for seven days. A woman has a blood pressure reading that's barely detectable. She is so ill, the paramedics are concerned that she may not survive a trip to hospital

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Andi Oliver and Matt Tebbutt journey through the islands of Barbados, Saint Lucia and Dominica to unearth the secrets of Caribbean food. In Barbados, they learn about rum production at Mount Gay, before finding out why hunting and eating lionfish could be good for the local ecosystem. In Saint Lucia the pair discover red bananas at a local market and head to Hotel Chocolat's Rabot Estate to find out why chocolate's flavour depends on where it is grown. They take a ferry to Dominica and discover a bread made by the Kalinago people with only the cassava root. Then, the duo meet a passion fruit farmer and discover why the fruit has such an evocative name

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole restores two quad bikes and a stylish sun lounger, while Simon O'Brien rescues a holed canoe and turns an old oak beam into a spice rack

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Essex, where Henry restores a car trailer and a 1970s racing bike, and Simon turns his attention to lovingly restoring a vintage model aircraft

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Engaged couple Rosie Lewis and Dexter Green get the competition underway, welcoming their rivals to the Bae Abermaw Hotel in Barmouth, north Wales. Their guests are wowed by the views from their rooms, but Tracy finds herself in a hairy situation. At dinner, Rosie and Dexter's romantic revelations and confessions surprise their guests. Their breakfast sets the standard for the rest of the week, while Tracy sets herself up as the one to watch, with some observations about her room that she's keen to bring to everyone's attention

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Rigsby's Guest House in Doncaster, South Yorkshire, run by Tracy Ward, whose pricing system causes problems for a couple of guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Pub landlord John Adams and his friend Dennis Gowie welcome their rivals to The Somerset Arms in Semington, Wiltshire, where Rosie and Dexter have some cleanliness issues in their room

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Nici Van Valen welcomes the contestants to the Cross Keys Hotel in Saffron Walden, Essex, for the final visit of the week, but a mark on Tracy's towel leaves a stain on her stay and a revelation at dinner leads to tension

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day of the competition sees the B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and to settle some scores. The Bae Abermaw Hotel is first and hosts Rosie and Dexter want to get to the bottom of Tracy's broken bath issue

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: First aid manager Jacqui hosts the first dinner party in Perth, central Scotland, where she plans to impress her guests, optometric consultant Daniel, cabinet-maker Craig, administrator Lorea and procurement officer Jenna with her hearty cuisine, including a sticky toffee pudding

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Aspiring actor Daniel hosts the second dinner party in Perth, and hopes to impress his fellow contestants with a twist on the traditional Scottish menu - serving spaghetti hagginaise for his main and soft-drink flavoured ice-cream for dessert. But with outspoken guest Jenna admitting her dislike for the host, the attention is diverted away from the food

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: For the third party from Perth, Lorea is on a mission to showcase the culinary delights of her native home in northern Spain, and her fiesta commences with a pink cocktail and an exotic quartet flown over from the Basque country especially to impress. She reveals that she used to model in the nude, which perplexes her guests, and there are tensions at bay between Daniel and Jenna, which threaten to spoil the evening

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Procurement officer Jenna hosts the fourth night of the competition in Perth, and tries to deliver the perfect evening. However, she struggles to hide her feelings toward aspiring actor Daniel after they have bickered all week, despite first aid manager Jacqui's attempts at reconciliation

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the final day in Perth, and Kiwi Craig's turn to throw a dinner party before the winner is named. He hopes that his land and sea menu of prime lobster and wild boar will see him score highly, but hadn't counted on Jenna and Craig's tiara quarrel from the night before raging on into his evening. The host tries his best to calm the couple, but tensions mount to the point where cutlery is slammed down and one guest storms out

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The latest competition kicks off in Harrogate, North Yorkshire, where management team Chris 'Clarkey' Clarke and Katie Light help run the 64-bedroom White Hart Hotel. When their guests arrive, Raouf and Chrissie from the New Promenade Hotel find out that they're staying in an outbuilding, while Robin and Liza Watson from Tall John's House love the modern gadgets in their room. In the afternoon, the guests meet some llamas and participate in a parade competition. Vicky from The Terrace makes an instant animal connection, which leaves quite an impression on her rivals

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Raouf Elnagaar and his friend Chrissie Greenwood welcome their rivals to the budget option New Promenade Hotel in Blackpool. When they check out their rooms, Vicky finds a teetering toilet seat, Robin and Liza fall out of love with the budget feel and Clarkey and Katie are astonished to find filth on the taps. In the afternoon, the guests ride Blackpool's famous historic tram, but first they must get it ready for service with a wash and a scrub. Robin reveals his impressive family lineage when he spots the Blackpool Tower. At dinner, Raouf reveals his sensitive side, but his experience in hospitality fails to show

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: For the third visit of the week, Vicky Crawford welcomes her rivals to her three-bedroom terraced house in Oxford, aptly named The Terrace. But two pairs of guests discover an array of filthy finds, including thick dust, hairs and a pre-sucked sweet. In the afternoon the guests go punting, but Raouf's childish mood spoils their time on the river

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The fourth and final visit is to Tall John's House, Robin and Liza Watson's grand Georgian mansion in the Welsh countryside, where the guests are wowed by the historic building and rooms. Raouf, Chrissie, Clarkey and Katie dig deep to find fault but can only find small problems. Following an afternoon in the billiard room, the guests gush over their friendship at dinner but, when it comes to the question of future meet-ups, Vicky drops a bombshell

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: White Hart Hotel management team Clarkey and Katie raise the issue of paying for their car park as the B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer revisit successful buyers from seasons past. In 2017 mother and daughter Linda and Laura bought a flat in Bristol with Phil's help that would suit Laura and her partner until they found their own home and Linda would move in with her husband when they retired. In 2018 Ranald and Marjorie were looking for a bigger home to hold family get-togethers with enough bedrooms for all the grandkids. Now Kirstie and Phil are back to see if the properties they bought were everything they hoped for

programme.title: PopMaster TV
programme.desc: Ken Bruce hosts the third heat, as more music enthusiasts from across the country are put to the test, from one hit wonders to chart-topping legends

programme.title: Kevin McCloud's Listed Britain
programme.desc: Kevin goes behind the scenes at Chatsworth House, and visits Castell Coch, the Marquess of Bute's Victorian gothic fantasy, complete with painted monkeys and medieval extravagance. Kevin also takes a look at the Palace of Westminster, one of the nation's treasured icons, but now badly in need of repair

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Fifty-one-year-old Mark is brought in after coming off his bike and doctors are concerned he may have suffered life-changing damage to his spine, with wife Karen rushing to be by his side. Ten-year-old Aiden comes to paediatrics with his mum and her girlfriend Lorraine, after hurting his foot on the monkey bars at school

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Neil, 53, is rushed into hospital with severe chest and lung injuries after coming off his bike, and 40-year-old Michael is brought in with chest pain and a heart rate of up to 180bpm after collapsing on a golf course. Lastly, doctors treat 27-year-old George, who accidentally grazed his eye on a low hanging branch

programme.title: Kevin McCloud's Listed Britain
programme.desc: Kevin goes behind the scenes at Chatsworth House, and visits Castell Coch, the Marquess of Bute's Victorian gothic fantasy, complete with painted monkeys and medieval extravagance. Kevin also takes a look at the Palace of Westminster, one of the nation's treasured icons, but now badly in need of repair

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Fifty-one-year-old Mark is brought in after coming off his bike and doctors are concerned he may have suffered life-changing damage to his spine, with wife Karen rushing to be by his side. Ten-year-old Aiden comes to paediatrics with his mum and her girlfriend Lorraine, after hurting his foot on the monkey bars at school

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Three boys are admitted for very different reasons, including cyclist Ollie, who arrives with head, neck and facial injuries having been involved in a traffic accident. Cameras also follow Dominic, another 16 year old. He was recovering at home after having his tonsils out when his throat started bleeding and he had to go to hospital

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton and John Whaite go on an incredible journey to unearth the origins of the K-foods that have exploded in popularity in recent years. Starting in Seoul, they uncover the crispy secrets of Korean Fried Chicken and reveal the key ingredient in Gochujang sauce. Continuing south to Icheon, they investigate a rice-based alcoholic spirit that outsells all others in Korea. In Cheonan they discover the reason for the massive popularity of pork luncheon meat - aka spam - and find out how it's made. A short detour to the beach at Dacheon leads to a surprising and unforgettable seafood with a nickname fit for the playground. Finally, in Gwangju, they enter a world of kimchi, and learn the key to its global expansion

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Samuel, 24, is rushed to A&E after crashing his motorbike and being thrown into the road, while two-year-old Florence is brought in by her mum and dad, who suspect that she has swallowed a coin. Seventy-year-old Clive arrives after struggling to breathe at home, but his wife Linda has a difficult time keeping him in check, as he likes to tell a joke or two. Documentary following life inside the A&E department of St George's in south-west London, focusing on patients, their families and the staff

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Rich, 35, is airlifted to the hospital after being involved in a high-speed collision on the M25, and doctors are concerned when they discover he has lost feeling below his chest and can't move his legs. Meanwhile, 36-year-old Justin has had a life-threatening accident while playing football and he is put into an induced coma while a CT scan is carried out, which reveals a potentially life-threatening bleed on his brain

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: This edition focuses on a mother's role in different family units. Jacob, 20, arrives in resus with suspected internal bleeding after crashing his car into a wall at high speed, while Joan, 82, is treated for a head wound after tripping down the stairs at home, and seven-year-old Jackson has a suspected infection on his cheek. Documentary following life inside the A&E department of St George's in south-west London, focusing on patients, their families and the staff dealing with the emergencies

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty and Matt Tebbutt embark on an extraordinary road trip through America's Deep South. Starting on the Gulf Coast, in New Orleans, they discover how the shells from the huge number of oysters consumed locally could protect the coastline. Heading through Mississippi, Jimmy and Matt find out what makes southern sweet tea different from a British cuppa. In Alabama, Matt wants to know why they fry green tomatoes, and Jimmy uncovers exactly what grits are. Then it's off to Tennessee to visit the Jack Daniels distillery and discover the secrets of sour mash. In Louisville, where the South meets the North, Jimmy and Matt learn about fast-food consistency at Kentucky Fried Chicken HQ

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Northamptonshire in search of more upcycling prospects. Simon finds some night vision goggles and an antique snooker table that's seen better days, while Henry chooses an enormous US army truck as one of his picks

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien search for more items to fix up and sell on, visiting a Norman castle, restoring a Fiat 500 and making a table from part of Southend pier

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition starts at Chimneys B&B in Holbrook in Suffolk, where owner Carolyn Spilling, who's joined by her friend Delia Duell, hopes that her uptempo personality will be a hit with the guests. However, guests Andrea and John are unimpressed to discover that they're in self-catering. At breakfast, Italian cousins Massimo and Raf aren't keen on the home from home approach

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to Neale and Julie Taylor's vegetarian B&B, Copperfields Guest House in Broadstairs, Kent. Carolyn and Delia give their room a thorough going over, while parking causes concerns for Andrea and John. The guests get competitive when they make bird boxes in the afternoon and at dinner, Neale and Julie make a revelation about a secret breakfast dish that intrigues chef Massimo

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at The George and Abbotsford in Melrose, Scotland, where Andria Gardner and John Marr aim to pull out all the stops to impress their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests spend the night at Massimo Perrulli's hotel in County Durham, where one couple have cleanliness concerns, another are missing a bath and the third have an axe to grind

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet to find out what they have been paid and settle some scores. Chimneys B&B's Carolyn and her friend Delia want some home truths from Massimo and Raf

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Sports fanatic James Cox hosts the first of the week's dinner parties in Northampton, and he is confident that his French-Italian menu will bag him the £1,000 prize. However, some of his guests - Barb Wiles, Donna Harris, Duncan Fielding and Caroline Nash - are left confused by his Vichyssoise soup starter, and the tiramisu causes one of them to choke

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Mother-of-three Donna Harris hosts the second dinner party in Northampton, and hopes that three courses of pure comfort-food will be enough to secure the £1,000 prize. Donna spends almost as much time fixing her make-up as she does preparing the meal, and when guests Duncan Fielding and Caroline Nash discover a secret stash of football shirts, they realise there is more to their hostess than meets the eye

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Part-time actress Caroline Nash is the host on day three of the competition in Northampton, and she hopes that a menu based on her travels will dazzle her guests. However, the night gets off to a shaky start as Donna Harris finds a hair on the side of her goat's cheese salad, while businessman Duncan Fielding seems more interested in goats themselves than the food

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Party-loving Barb Wiles hosts the fourth dinner party in the Northampton area, and hopes her Australian-themed menu will wow her guests and win her the £1,000 prize. However, the competitors are unnerved by her interest in extraterrestrials while snooping around her bedroom, and things go from bad to worse when a seafood starter and undercooked chicken result in tears at the dinner table

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Attention-loving businessman and keen cricketer Duncan Fielding takes centre stage in hosting the final dinner party from Northampton. He creates a menu based on the story of his life - which is full of surprises and full of spice - but it proves a challenge for three of his guests, who like their food mild. His main feature is a chicken and reindeer curry, and the table is impressed to discover that he runs a community pub from the bottom of his garden

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at the White Horse Inn in Maldon, Essex, where owners Jayne and Pete Kocinoga hope their attention to detail will win over their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to Barnfield B&B in St Osyth, Essex, run by Christine and Paul, where as well as offering rooms, Christine also runs spiritual retreats

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: For the third visit of the week the contestants head to The Black Swan Inn in Pickering, North Yorkshire, where husband and wife Phil and Jill Hall hope to impress their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stay is at Delamere Farm near Peterborough, where proud owner Agi Chapman is sent into a spin after hearing some critical comments over breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day arrives, and the hosts of The White Horse Inn, Barnfield B&B, The Black Swan Inn and Delamere Farm all discover just how much their guests valued their services

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Phil Spencer catches up with two sets of discerning house-hunters, each with very strong ideas on what they wanted. The searches may have been tough - in some instances they didn't even make it inside some of the properties - but both have faced even more challenging times since. In 2012, couple Robin and Colin wanted to move outside of London, but only an architectural gem was going to do. In 2014, Lia and Andrew said they were done renting in London's popular Isle of Dogs, and wanted to buy their own home now they were starting a family

programme.title: PopMaster TV
programme.desc: Ken Bruce hosts Britain's toughest music quiz, as five more players compete in the second heat, in six rounds, plus Original PopMaster and Three in Ten. The players with the highest scores across the series will secure a place in the Grand Final

programme.title: Ancient Greece by Train with Alice Roberts
programme.desc: The historian takes to the railways of Greece and Turkey as she learns about the Ancient Greek civilisation. Her journey begins in the northern Greek city of Thessaloniki, home of Alexander the Great, and visits a museum featuring a burial chamber for ancient Greek royalty. She heads south to Delphi to see the magnificent ruins of what is possibly the greatest ancient site in the world

programme.title: Castle Secrets
programme.sub-title: Nazi Crypt, the Cannibal's Caves, Hall of Screams
programme.desc: Delving into the stories behind historical fortifications. This edition explores the depths of a ruined castle where Hitler's dark occult dealings are revealed. Plus, learning about a Scottish clifftop bastion with a long history of criminal activity and a palace, which acted as a stage for betrayals

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A baby has a dangerously high temperature and the paramedics suspect sepsis, and a woman who is pregnant and has tested positive for Covid-19 has had a migraine for seven days. A woman has a blood pressure reading that's barely detectable. She is so ill, the paramedics are concerned that she may not survive a trip to hospital

programme.title: Kevin McCloud's Listed Britain
programme.desc: Kevin goes behind the scenes at Chatsworth House, and visits Castell Coch, the Marquess of Bute's Victorian gothic fantasy, complete with painted monkeys and medieval extravagance. Kevin also takes a look at the Palace of Westminster, one of the nation's treasured icons, but now badly in need of repair

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Twenty-five-year-old Billy arrives at St George's by helicopter after being involved in a motorcycle accident in which a van ran over his chest. The team work quickly to get him into CT to see if he has any life-changing injuries, while his girlfriend, mum and stepdad desperately try to get to the hospital. Meanwhile, 58-year-old Stephen comes into A&E with his wife and son after showing signs of a stroke, and Milly brings her two-year-old son Freddie in after he jammed his thumb in a door

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A baby has a dangerously high temperature and the paramedics suspect sepsis, and a woman who is pregnant and has tested positive for Covid-19 has had a migraine for seven days. A woman has a blood pressure reading that's barely detectable. She is so ill, the paramedics are concerned that she may not survive a trip to hospital

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Andi Oliver and Matt Tebbutt journey through the islands of Barbados, Saint Lucia and Dominica to unearth the secrets of Caribbean food. In Barbados, they learn about rum production at Mount Gay, before finding out why hunting and eating lionfish could be good for the local ecosystem. In Saint Lucia the pair discover red bananas at a local market and head to Hotel Chocolat's Rabot Estate to find out why chocolate's flavour depends on where it is grown. They take a ferry to Dominica and discover a bread made by the Kalinago people with only the cassava root. Then, the duo meet a passion fruit farmer and discover why the fruit has such an evocative name

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole restores two quad bikes and a stylish sun lounger, while Simon O'Brien rescues a holed canoe and turns an old oak beam into a spice rack

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Essex, where Henry restores a car trailer and a 1970s racing bike, and Simon turns his attention to lovingly restoring a vintage model aircraft

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Engaged couple Rosie Lewis and Dexter Green get the competition underway, welcoming their rivals to the Bae Abermaw Hotel in Barmouth, north Wales. Their guests are wowed by the views from their rooms, but Tracy finds herself in a hairy situation. At dinner, Rosie and Dexter's romantic revelations and confessions surprise their guests. Their breakfast sets the standard for the rest of the week, while Tracy sets herself up as the one to watch, with some observations about her room that she's keen to bring to everyone's attention

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Rigsby's Guest House in Doncaster, South Yorkshire, run by Tracy Ward, whose pricing system causes problems for a couple of guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Pub landlord John Adams and his friend Dennis Gowie welcome their rivals to The Somerset Arms in Semington, Wiltshire, where Rosie and Dexter have some cleanliness issues in their room

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Nici Van Valen welcomes the contestants to the Cross Keys Hotel in Saffron Walden, Essex, for the final visit of the week, but a mark on Tracy's towel leaves a stain on her stay and a revelation at dinner leads to tension

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day of the competition sees the B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and to settle some scores. The Bae Abermaw Hotel is first and hosts Rosie and Dexter want to get to the bottom of Tracy's broken bath issue

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: First aid manager Jacqui hosts the first dinner party in Perth, central Scotland, where she plans to impress her guests, optometric consultant Daniel, cabinet-maker Craig, administrator Lorea and procurement officer Jenna with her hearty cuisine, including a sticky toffee pudding

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Aspiring actor Daniel hosts the second dinner party in Perth, and hopes to impress his fellow contestants with a twist on the traditional Scottish menu - serving spaghetti hagginaise for his main and soft-drink flavoured ice-cream for dessert. But with outspoken guest Jenna admitting her dislike for the host, the attention is diverted away from the food

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: For the third party from Perth, Lorea is on a mission to showcase the culinary delights of her native home in northern Spain, and her fiesta commences with a pink cocktail and an exotic quartet flown over from the Basque country especially to impress. She reveals that she used to model in the nude, which perplexes her guests, and there are tensions at bay between Daniel and Jenna, which threaten to spoil the evening

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Procurement officer Jenna hosts the fourth night of the competition in Perth, and tries to deliver the perfect evening. However, she struggles to hide her feelings toward aspiring actor Daniel after they have bickered all week, despite first aid manager Jacqui's attempts at reconciliation

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the final day in Perth, and Kiwi Craig's turn to throw a dinner party before the winner is named. He hopes that his land and sea menu of prime lobster and wild boar will see him score highly, but hadn't counted on Jenna and Craig's tiara quarrel from the night before raging on into his evening. The host tries his best to calm the couple, but tensions mount to the point where cutlery is slammed down and one guest storms out

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The latest competition kicks off in Harrogate, North Yorkshire, where management team Chris 'Clarkey' Clarke and Katie Light help run the 64-bedroom White Hart Hotel. When their guests arrive, Raouf and Chrissie from the New Promenade Hotel find out that they're staying in an outbuilding, while Robin and Liza Watson from Tall John's House love the modern gadgets in their room. In the afternoon, the guests meet some llamas and participate in a parade competition. Vicky from The Terrace makes an instant animal connection, which leaves quite an impression on her rivals

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Raouf Elnagaar and his friend Chrissie Greenwood welcome their rivals to the budget option New Promenade Hotel in Blackpool. When they check out their rooms, Vicky finds a teetering toilet seat, Robin and Liza fall out of love with the budget feel and Clarkey and Katie are astonished to find filth on the taps. In the afternoon, the guests ride Blackpool's famous historic tram, but first they must get it ready for service with a wash and a scrub. Robin reveals his impressive family lineage when he spots the Blackpool Tower. At dinner, Raouf reveals his sensitive side, but his experience in hospitality fails to show

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: For the third visit of the week, Vicky Crawford welcomes her rivals to her three-bedroom terraced house in Oxford, aptly named The Terrace. But two pairs of guests discover an array of filthy finds, including thick dust, hairs and a pre-sucked sweet. In the afternoon the guests go punting, but Raouf's childish mood spoils their time on the river

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The fourth and final visit is to Tall John's House, Robin and Liza Watson's grand Georgian mansion in the Welsh countryside, where the guests are wowed by the historic building and rooms. Raouf, Chrissie, Clarkey and Katie dig deep to find fault but can only find small problems. Following an afternoon in the billiard room, the guests gush over their friendship at dinner but, when it comes to the question of future meet-ups, Vicky drops a bombshell

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: White Hart Hotel management team Clarkey and Katie raise the issue of paying for their car park as the B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer revisit successful buyers from seasons past. In 2017 mother and daughter Linda and Laura bought a flat in Bristol with Phil's help that would suit Laura and her partner until they found their own home and Linda would move in with her husband when they retired. In 2018 Ranald and Marjorie were looking for a bigger home to hold family get-togethers with enough bedrooms for all the grandkids. Now Kirstie and Phil are back to see if the properties they bought were everything they hoped for

programme.title: United States of Animals

programme.title: Secrets of the Bees
programme.desc: National Geographic Explorer Bertie Gregory reveals the hidden world of bees and the extraordinary secrets behind their success. From the broomstick bee's ingenious architecture to the meat-eating bee of the Amazon rainforest, their remarkable talents are uncovered alongside new insights into bee intelligence. Through the life of one honeybee, follow her bustling hive as her sisters race to survive the winter.

programme.title: Secrets of the Bees
programme.desc: National Geographic Explorer Bertie Gregory returns to the honeybee sisterhood as they race to rebuild before winter. Across the planet, other bees face challenges of their own, from Dawson's bees battling for mates to honeybees facing the global spread of varroa mites. With Explorer Dr. Sammy Ramsey, Bertie explores the mounting pressures on bees and the bold solutions to secure their future.

programme.title: World of Stonehenge
programme.desc: Neil Oliver explores the origins of Britain and its people, a story forged over thousands of years. In south Wales, he joins a team of archaeologists to examine 8,000-year-old footprints revealed beneath tidal mud banks, and abseils into a cave dating back 33,000 years. The historian also reveals how, as the last ice age receded some 11,000 years ago, Britain became an island, a fate that was sealed following a cataclysmic tsunami around 6,000 BC

programme.title: The Stuarts
programme.sub-title: And I Will Make Them One Nation
programme.desc: Clare Jackson examines the history of the Stuarts, who grappled with the chaos of three separate kingdoms, multiple religions and civil war during the 17th century. In the opening episode, she looks at James VI of Scotland's attempts to unite his country with England and Ireland and persuade his subjects to feel more British

programme.title: WWII in Colour: Road to Victory
programme.desc: In September 1939 a German U-boat fires its first torpedo, setting in motion the longest continuous battle of the war. A cat and mouse game of epic proportions ensues as German U-boats and allied forces engage in a five-year battle for supremacy in the Atlantic

programme.title: Impossible Peace
programme.desc: An insight into the time of peace between the First and Second World Wars, how 20 years of peace and the forces reshaping the world produced another war

programme.title: Plunderer: The Life and Times of a Nazi Art Thief
programme.desc: Part one of two. Jonathan Petropoulos investigates the life of former Nazi art dealer Bruno Lohse, who became Hermann Göring's personal collector, tasked with finding desirable works of art. After the Second World War, Lohse spent a brief time in prison but later resumed his career as a dealer, often trading in art looted during the conflict

programme.title: WWII in Colour: Road to Victory
programme.desc: With Britain standing alone, Churchill realises maintaining control of the vital oil supply line through the Suez Canal is critical to his nation?s hopes. After the fall of the strategically important port of Tobruk, Rommel only has the defensive line at El Alamein left to cross. Britain?s General Montgomery drives Rommel back from El Alamein, but that is only the beginning; America?s rookie troops make landfall in North Africa and join the fight

programme.title: Weathered: Earth's Extremes
programme.desc: Maiya May explores heat in Portland, Oregon, and Medellín, Colombia, a city that is leading the revolution against sweltering city temperatures before visiting the hottest city in the US, Phoenix, Arizona

programme.title: World of Stonehenge
programme.desc: Neil Oliver examines what impact the arrival of farming had on ancient social structures. He dives off the Isle of Wight where archaeologists have found an 8,000-year-old hunter settlement and learns what happened when hunter-gatherers met farmers for the first time. The historian reveals that when cultivation eventually came to Britain around 4,000 BC, it brought about the Neolithic era and a deep set of changes in people's way of life, from ground-breaking inventions like pottery, to new belief systems

programme.title: The Stuarts
programme.desc: After Charles I's disastrous attempt to impose political and religious uniformity throughout England, Scotland and Ireland, both the Stuart dynasty and its three kingdoms fell into an abyss. In this episode, Dr Clare Jackson reveals how the unprecedented religious violence of the Wars of the Three Kingdoms shaped British political culture and subsequent constitutional crises

programme.title: WWII in Colour: Road to Victory
programme.desc: With Britain standing alone, Churchill realises maintaining control of the vital oil supply line through the Suez Canal is critical to his nation?s hopes. programme.title: Impossible Peace
programme.desc: As the US and Great Britain began to increase their military holdings while keeping their vanquished foes under-armed, it was at the expense of a humiliated Germany

programme.title: Plunderer: The Life and Times of a Nazi Art Thief
programme.desc: Part two of two. Jonathan Petropoulos investigates Nazi art dealer Bruno Lohse's life in post-war America. Dealing in looted art, he became an invisible hand, even as the original owners' families sought the return of works rightfully theirs. Featuring an archive interviews with Lohse himself, as well as curators, investigators and descendants of the victims of Nazi art theft

programme.title: World of Stonehenge
programme.desc: Neil Oliver delves into the mysteries of the Stone Age and the meaning of the sacred monuments that were built throughout Britain and Ireland. The historian also joins people arriving in Stonehenge to celebrate the mid-summer sunrise, and learns about the religious and sociological significance of the monument

Their transportation is the oldest car ever to grace the trip, a 1932 Riley Monaco. Charles unearths a 19th-century Japanese find, and some items from closer to home, but further back in time. Natasha relies on some obscure primary-school knowledge to decode one of her finds

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell follow three stories of siblings brought together after decades apart. Last year the programme introduced John Nicklin to four siblings on his birth mother's side, and in the meantime he has uncovered secrets on his father's side of his family, leading to a whole new reunion. They also catch up brother and sister Tracey Collins and Christian Hilton, who reunited in 2014, as well as 81-year-old Pauline Wood as she prepares for her wedding day, with long-lost brother Colin set to give her away

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell revisit more of the programme's most memorable searches, catching up with two people whose lives have been transformed. programme.title: Long Lost Family

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: BBC world affairs editor John Simpson always thought his great-grandfather was Samuel F Cody, the Wild West entertainer who also achieved fame as the first person in Britain to conduct a powered flight. It was only later that he discovered his great-grandmother had run off with Cody, taking her children with her. John follows the trail of his adventurous relative and her life with the cowboy-turned-aviator. He also investigates what happened to the husband she left behind - John's real great-grandfather - a search that leads to a shocking discovery

programme.title: EastEnders

programme.title: Donal's Real Time Recipes
programme.desc: Donal Skehan proves that going meat-free doesn't mean missing out, serving up vegetarian dishes that are anything but boring. He starts with a bowl of dal, topped with golden, crispy cauliflower, sticky tofu rice bowls, and crispy gnocchi tossed with minty peas and finished with a spoonful of mascarpone

programme.title: How to Cook Well in Morocco
programme.desc: Rory O'Connell learns the art of cooking couscous at the Kasbah Bab Ourika, a boutique hotel at the foothills of the Atlas Mountains, which stretch across North West Africa, through Morocco, Algeria and Tunisia. Argan trees only grow natively in this part of Morocco and Rory visits the Bio-Aromatic Garden of Ourika to see how Argan nuts are heated and ground to produce argan oil

programme.title: RTÉ News: Nine O'Clock and Weather
programme.desc: The latest national and international news

programme.title: Uncharted with Ray Goggins
programme.desc: Ray Goggins challenges Olympian Ciara Mageean and Dublin football star Michael Darragh MacAuley to a test of endurance across the Costa Rica. The duo must make their way across the entire country by every means possible, starting by skydiving into the tropical rainforest. From there, Ciara and Michael have to cycle, run, kayak and trek across 250km of the world's most majestic but challenging terrain

programme.title: Maigret
programme.desc: The detective anticipates the robbers' next move and gets the chance to confront Cuendet's killers. But it seems the crooks have a masteplan that no one could have imagined. Crime drama inspired by the novels of Georges Simenon, starring Benjamin Wainwright

programme.title: Swindlers
programme.desc: The stories of some of Ireland's most renowned con artists and white collar criminals, told by their victims, friends and colleagues they deceived, journalists that covered the cases and the law enforcers who brought them to justice. This edition looks at international con artist, bigamist and sweet-talking criminal Julia Holmes from Tyrone

programme.title: Meat Loaf: From Hell and to Connaught
programme.desc: Documentary about the larger-than-life US rock star's 1990 visit to Ireland, when he performed in some of the country's most unlikely venues

programme.title: Welcome To Moore Street
programme.desc: Capturing life on one of Dublin's most iconic streets, sharing colourful stories from the local traders who made the street famous and the immigrants from across the world who have made Ireland their home. Sri Lankan chef Naleem opens a restaurant in the Moore Street Mall and reiki healer Amanda chases her ambitions

programme.title: High Road, Low Road
programme.desc: Radio presenters Zeinab Elguzouli and Mikey O'Reilly travel to Prague, where one enjoys a first-class luxury experience and the other explores the same destination on a budget

programme.title: Build Your Own Home
programme.desc: Master builder Harrison Gardner helps newly married couple Aoife NicChraith and Louis Brennan, who have plans for an ambitious modern extension to their one-bedroom cottage in Tuam, County Galway. They are essentially attaching a new three-bedroom house to a 200-year-old house to cater for their growing family

programme.title: The Irish for Sex
programme.desc: Comedian Bláithín de Burca searches nationwide to find words for women's bodies and sexuality in Gaeilge. She explores the Irish language of the past, present and future to find womens' words in the hopes of unlocking a uniquely Irish experience of womanhood. Featuring interviews with guests, including Manchán Magan, Dr Sharon Arbuthnot of Cambridge University and The Wild Geeze

programme.desc: An analysis of the life and work of JMW Turner as two enthusiasts are joined by art historians and academics to reveal the hidden codes they believe are woven into his paintings

programme.title: Pet Shop Boys: Dreamworld - The Greatest Hits: Live in Copenhagen
programme.desc: A 2023 concert at the Royal Arena in the Danish capital, with Neil Tennant and Chris Lowe performing hits including West End Girls, Suburbia, Rent, Domino Dancing and It's a Sin

programme.title: Queen: From Rags to Rhapsody
programme.desc: Documentary recalling the rock band's early years, spent struggling gigging at pubs and colleges, and following their progress up to the moment they captured the UK's hearts and minds with what was to become one of the greatest songs of all time - Bohemian Rhapsody. The documentary features contributions by band members and outtakes from recording sessions

programme.title: David Hockney: A Celebration
programme.desc: A profile of the artist, featuring interviews with him in London and Normandy, and footage of him at work

programme.title: Cold War & Cinema
programme.desc: A look at depictions of the era on film, beginning as America and the Soviet Union fought as allies toward the end of the Second World War, but tension still remained

programme.title: Stories from the National Portrait Gallery
programme.desc: Kate Bryan explores portraitures from the 18th and 19th centuries, whlle comedian and artist Jim Moir, aka Vic Reeves, drops in to discuss his favourite work in the gallery

programme.title: André Rieu: Welcome to My World
programme.desc: The violinist and his Johann Strauss Orchestra perform Conquest of Paradise, La Vergine and I Will Survive with a 150-strong male voice choir

programme.title: Classic FM Live - Great British Classics
programme.desc: A concert at the Royal Albert Hall in which Ben Palmer conducts the Philharmonia Orchestra in a selection of music by British composers, including Vaughan Williams and Elgar

programme.title: Artist of the Year: Masterclass
programme.desc: Tutorials on ways to develop a narrative in portraiture. Tai-Shan Schierenberg has tips on painting portraits

programme.title: Artist of the Year: Masterclass
programme.desc: A tutorial on improvising with watercolour. Tai-Shan Schierenberg has tips on painting portraits

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: When the new vicar of a church plans radical changes, conflict arises over a secret one of the locals has kept for years

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A fencing contest in London lands naive Nicholas Lillie in the middle of a bizarre adventure. Drama, with Benedict Taylor and Jim Broadbent

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents
programme.desc: A gun shop owner sees two mobsters murdering a witness from another crime in 1927 Chicago and is afraid he will share the same fate. With Art Carney and Robert Bray

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents
programme.desc: Lucy Pryor allows herself to be hypnotised at a house party by Miles Farnham. While under his spell, she picks up a pair of scissors and kills her husband, apparently influenced by the spirit of a woman who committed the same crime more than 100 years before. With Phyllis Thaxter and Tom Helmore

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: The life and career of the English actor and film-maker, who starred in Brighton Rock, 10 Rillington Place, The Great Escape and Jurassic Park and won an Oscar for directing Gandhi

programme.title: André Rieu: Welcome to My World
programme.sub-title: Andre Rieu: Waltz Around the World
programme.desc: The violinist showcases his music as he and his orchestra travel the world, visiting places including Malta, Scotland and Ireland

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A woman joins a dating agency to try to overcome her shyness, but her plan backfires when an out-of-town solicitor tries a one-night stand

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: An American thief faces extradition from a Middle East country for a crime he did not commit. Michael Brandon stars

programme.title: Stories from the National Portrait Gallery
programme.desc: Kate Bryan explores portraitures from the 18th and 19th centuries, whlle comedian and artist Jim Moir, aka Vic Reeves, drops in to discuss his favourite work in the gallery

programme.title: Classic Albums
programme.sub-title: Sex Pistols - Never Mind the B*******
programme.desc: The story behind the Sex Pistols' sole studio album, 1977's Never Mind the B*******, Here's the Sex Pistols. Featuring interviews with singer John Lydon and manager Malcolm McLaren

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: A profile of the English actor, who was nominated for an Oscar for The Elephant Man and starred in 10 Rillington Place, Alien, Midnight Express and Nineteen Eighty-Four

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year
programme.sub-title: Noel Fielding, Reggie Yates and Daphne Selfe
programme.desc: The contestants paint celebrity sitters Noel Fielding, Reggie Yates and Daphne Selfe, before the judges decide who to send through to the next round of the competition

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A romantic Frenchman is forced into a decision when his mistress falls pregnant

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A woman discovers the disturbing secret of an archaeologist able to immediately date relics with unerring accuracy

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents
programme.desc: The mistress of a married man meets his wife at a restaurant to request that she grant her husband a divorce, but the wife stubbornly refuses resulting in a bit of intrigue involving the wife's unsavoury past. With Margaret Leighton and Marsha Hunt

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents
programme.desc: A killer on the run demands help from two cantankerous Western prospectors, when his car breaks down near their remote shack. The old couple already face eviction unless they can demonstrate that they are homesteading, but in a patch of Nevada desert which can barely support a juniper tree, how can they? With William Demarest and Roscoe Ates

programme.title: Matisse: The Last Masterpiece
programme.desc: A look at the story of the creation of the Chapelle du Rosaire in the French commune of Vence, revealing how Henri Matisse was inspired by nurse Monique Bourgeois

programme.title: Backstage with the London Philharmonic Orchestra
programme.desc: The orchestra performs Mahler's Second Symphony at London's Royal Festival Hall for the opening concert of their 2023/2024 season

programme.title: The Seventies
programme.desc: A look at how American popular music exploded into new formats, while artists including Blondie, Billy Joel and Kiss found new ways to express themselves

programme.title: Rolling Stone: Stories from the Edge
programme.desc: A look at the magazine's explosive 1993 interview with Bill Clinton, its embracing of hip-hop, and the 2008 cover story Britney Spears: Inside an American Tragedy

programme.title: Katharine Hepburn: Call Me Kate
programme.desc: Documentary capturing the actress's spirit and determination, combining new and archive footage to tell the story of a true Hollywood star who won four Academy Awards

programme.title: Lily and Lolly: The Forgotten Yeats Sisters
programme.desc: Imelda May explores the story of WB Yeats' two sisters Susan 'Lily' Yeats and Elizabeth 'Lolly' Yeats, who played a significant role in the revival of Irish literature in the 1920s

programme.title: Stories from the National Portrait Gallery
programme.desc: Kate Bryan explores 20th-century portraiture, examining depictions of war poets and Queen Elizabeth II. Plus, Jim Moir drops in to discuss his favourite work

programme.desc: A 2023 concert by the German violinist in which she is joined by John Williams, Yefim Bronfman, Pablo Ferrández, Susanna Malkki and the Pittsburgh Symphony Orchestra

programme.title: Urban Secrets

programme.title: Richard E Grant's Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: The actor returns to explore more of the world's most luxurious hotels and begins in Miami's South Beach, home to some of the most hedonistic and extravagant establishments in America. At the Fontainebleau, he meets a man with eye-opening stories about Frank Sinatra's regular visits and gets a shock when he discovers how much it costs to hire a booth in the night club. Richard also gatecrashes swimwear fashion week at the Raleigh Hotel, where he speaks to the event manager about the many celebrity parties he has organised

programme.title: Richard E Grant's Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: In this edition the actor explores the luxury hotels of Hong Kong. Home to more skyscrapers than any other city, he first checks in to the Mandarin in the financial district, before experiencing the Four Seasons, the only hotel with two Michelin three-star resturants, as well as the timeless Peninsula in Kowloon, which even provides a Rolls-Royce airport taxi service. He ends his stay at the Ritz-Carlton, home to the world's highest swimming pool

programme.sub-title: The Heart of a Saturday Night
programme.desc: Lewis and Munch investigate a carjacking that led to a three-year-old girl going missing. Gritty police drama, starring Richard Belzer

programme.title: The Guest Wing
programme.desc: Documentary following stately home owners who hire out their estates to earn extra income, and the clients who pay to use their properties for functions including stag parties, weddings and hunting trips. The programme explores how the reality of life in Britain's grand houses is often less glamorous than it appears, as proprietors struggle to maintain crumbling buildings and pay utility bills, and examines the modern-day divide between domestic staff and their employers. Featuring life at Chillingham Castle, Maunsel House, Carlton Towers and Muncaster Castle

programme.title: The Guest Wing
programme.desc: Lord and Lady Fitzalan Howard, the owners of Carlton Towers near Selby in North Yorkshire, prepare to host a fitness boot camp weekend at their property - while also welcoming an array of guests to celebrate their son's birthday. Meanwhile, Sir Benjamin Slade hopes a murder mystery weekend at Maunsel House in Somerset will help pay off a sizeable electricity bill

programme.title: Richard E Grant's Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: The actor heads to Tokyo and checks out the Park Hyatt, the hotel featured in the film Lost in Translation, and goes behind the closed doors of the Japanese capital's love hotels. In the fashionable district of Meguro, Richard takes in the Claska, which was the city's first boutique hotel and has its own canine beauty salon

programme.title: Richard E Grant's Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: Richard E Grant travels to Venice, said to be the city with the highest concentration of luxury hotels on the planet. He discovers how a measuring error led to the building of the city's biggest swimming pool, tries out a 15,000 euro-a-night royal suite whose bed has been slept in by everyone from the Beckhams to Prince Charles and the Duchess of Cornwall, and visits a hotel that keeps its doors open even when water floods to knee height. He also finds out why breaking a window once earned Ernest Hemingway a 10 per cent discount on his room

programme.desc: A nervous Alma and Ellsworth get married, and Tolliver informs George Hearst of Wolcott's murderous acts. When the engineer is confronted, he takes drastic action with devastating consequences. Wu launches an attack on Lee

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.sub-title: Tell Your God to Ready for Blood
programme.desc: Seth Bullock and bartender Harry Manning compete for the office of sheriff, and EB Farnum and Sol Star campaign to become mayor. Meanwhile, Swearengen continues to view George Hearst with suspicion. Western drama set in a lawless US town, starring Ian McShane

programme.title: Billions
programme.desc: Mike Prince takes his place on the Axe Capital throne, while Chuck Rhoades questions how much wealth and power someone should have

programme.title: Billions
programme.desc: Prince instructs the trading floor on a new play but is met with scepticism. Taylor and Wendy try to nail down Prince's intentions

programme.title: My Brilliant Friend
programme.desc: After a tragic loss and stunned by Nino's actions yet again, Elena takes refuge at Lila's and finally learns the truth about him. In Italian

programme.title: The New Pope
programme.desc: Having uncovered John Paul III's secrets, Voiello begins his plan to regain power. Meanwhile, Lenny Belardo secretly returns to the Vatican

programme.title: Billions
programme.desc: Mike Prince takes his place on the Axe Capital throne, while Chuck Rhoades questions how much wealth and power someone should have

programme.title: Billions
programme.desc: Prince instructs the trading floor on a new play but is met with scepticism. Taylor and Wendy try to nail down Prince's intentions

programme.title: Task
programme.desc: As he prepares for an emotional court appearance, Tom buries himself in the case, while Emily questions her obligations to the family. Maeve makes a critical choice to protect her own loved ones, while Robbie works to find a way to turn his newfound surplus into cash

programme.title: Task
programme.desc: As Robbie and Cliff try to find a potential buyer, Tom and Grasso level with the Dark Hearts, and Aleah and Lizzie question Peaches' former co-workers. Later, a raid leads Tom to a shocking revelation

programme.title: The White Lotus
programme.desc: Saxon gets work calls, so Timothy enforces a no-phone rule, Chelsea faces danger after going to town with Rick, and Jaclyn pushes Laurie toward Valentin

programme.title: Euphoria
programme.desc: Drama offering a look at the complexities of life in a social-media obsessed world, starring Zendaya, Jacob Elordi and Sydney Sweeney

programme.title: Succession
programme.desc: As Kendall, Shiv and Roman hone their pitch for investors in Los Angeles, Logan learns of a rival bid. Drama, starring Brian Cox and Jeremy Strong

programme.title: Succession
programme.desc: While Kendall, Shiv and Roman consider an aggressive move regarding the Matsson deal, Connor tries to make the most of his wedding rehearsal dinner

programme.title: Task
programme.desc: In the midst of preparing for a key operation, Tom questions the loyalty of the members of his task force, while Robbie and Cliff plan for their meeting at the park. Perry searches for answers within the gang and uncovers a shocking truth and Maeve casts doubt on Robbie's exit plan

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.sub-title: The Heart of a Saturday Night
programme.desc: Lewis and Munch investigate a carjacking that led to a three-year-old girl going missing. Gritty police drama, starring Richard Belzer

programme.title: The Guest Wing
programme.desc: Documentary following stately home owners who hire out their estates to earn extra income, and the clients who pay to use their properties for functions including stag parties, weddings and hunting trips. The programme explores how the reality of life in Britain's grand houses is often less glamorous than it appears, as proprietors struggle to maintain crumbling buildings and pay utility bills, and examines the modern-day divide between domestic staff and their employers. Featuring life at Chillingham Castle, Maunsel House, Carlton Towers and Muncaster Castle

programme.title: The Guest Wing
programme.desc: Lord and Lady Fitzalan Howard, the owners of Carlton Towers near Selby in North Yorkshire, prepare to host a fitness boot camp weekend at their property - while also welcoming an array of guests to celebrate their son's birthday. Meanwhile, Sir Benjamin Slade hopes a murder mystery weekend at Maunsel House in Somerset will help pay off a sizeable electricity bill

programme.title: Richard E Grant's Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: The actor heads to Tokyo and checks out the Park Hyatt, the hotel featured in the film Lost in Translation, and goes behind the closed doors of the Japanese capital's love hotels. In the fashionable district of Meguro, Richard takes in the Claska, which was the city's first boutique hotel and has its own canine beauty salon

programme.title: Richard E Grant's Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: Richard E Grant travels to Venice, said to be the city with the highest concentration of luxury hotels on the planet. He discovers how a measuring error led to the building of the city's biggest swimming pool, tries out a 15,000 euro-a-night royal suite whose bed has been slept in by everyone from the Beckhams to Prince Charles and the Duchess of Cornwall, and visits a hotel that keeps its doors open even when water floods to knee height. He also finds out why breaking a window once earned Ernest Hemingway a 10 per cent discount on his room

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: An outstanding computer genius at a prestigious prep school is found stabbed to death

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: On Pembleton's first case back, he looks into a divorced woman who is found stabbed to death and her 10-year-old twin boys shot in the head

programme.title: Mean Girls (2004)
programme.desc: A teenager is surprised when she is befriended by the girls in the most popular clique at her new school. However, a dating mishap leads to trouble and her supposed friends start to turn on her. Urged on by other pals outside their circle, the newcomer resolves to bring down the self-appointed queen of campus - only to end up adopting some of her rival's crueller characteristics. Comedy, with Lindsay Lohan, Rachel McAdams, Tina Fey and Lacey Chabert

programme.title: Spanglish (2004)
programme.desc: A Mexican woman is offered a well-paid job as housekeeper to a wealthy American celebrity chef, and relocates with her daughter to Los Angeles - but her employer's neurotic wife causes all sorts of problems. Comedy directed by James L Brooks, starring Paz Vega, Adam Sandler, Tea Leoni and Cloris Leachman

programme.title: The Whole Nine Yards (2000)
programme.desc: Down-on-his-luck suburban dentist Nicholas Oseransky is horrified to discover his new neighbour is a former hitman, who is now in hiding after turning evidence against gangland bosses. When his wife convinces him to contact the Mob to try to extort money in exchange for revealing the crook's location, Nicholas finds himself out of his depth and in mortal danger. Comedy, with Matthew Perry, Bruce Willis, Rosanna Arquette and Michael Clarke Duncan

programme.title: Superbad (2007)
programme.desc: Two teenage friends are on the brink of going to different colleges, so decide to mark the event by going to a party where they hope to lose their virginity. However, they have promised to supply the booze for the event, a task that proves much harder than expected because they are under age. Unfortunately, when a friend steps in to help, the situation gets even worse. Comedy, starring Jonah Hill, Michael Cera and Christopher Mintz-Plasse

programme.title: Miss Congeniality (2000)
programme.desc: A feminist FBI agent is sent undercover as a contestant in the Miss United States pageant when the event becomes the target of a terrorist bomb threat. As she is not exactly catwalk material, a veteran British coach is brought on board to oversee her transformation into a glamorous beauty queen. Comedy, starring Sandra Bullock, Michael Caine, Benjamin Bratt, Candice Bergen and William Shatner

programme.title: Miss Congeniality 2: Armed and Fabulous (2005)
programme.desc: FBI agent Gracie Hart is taken off active duty and made the face of the bureau's publicity campaign, but would rather catch crooks than be the focus of media attention - so when her friends are kidnapped, she disobeys orders to run her own undercover investigation with the help of a short-tempered colleague. Comedy sequel, starring Sandra Bullock, Regina King, William Shatner and Heather Burns

programme.title: Mamma Mia! (2008)
programme.desc: A former member of a singing trio, now running a hotel on a Greek island, is reunited with her old bandmates for her daughter's wedding - but is surprised when three of her old flames also show up unexpectedly. The three men have been invited by the bride, who wants to find out which one of them is her father. Musical comedy, with a soundtrack of Abba songs, starring Meryl Streep, Pierce Brosnan, Julie Walters, Colin Firth, Stellan Skarsgard and Amanda Seyfried

programme.title: The Whole Nine Yards (2000)
programme.desc: Down-on-his-luck suburban dentist Nicholas Oseransky is horrified to discover his new neighbour is a former hitman, who is now in hiding after turning evidence against gangland bosses. When his wife convinces him to contact the Mob to try to extort money in exchange for revealing the crook's location, Nicholas finds himself out of his depth and in mortal danger. Comedy, with Matthew Perry, Bruce Willis, Rosanna Arquette and Michael Clarke Duncan

programme.title: Two Weeks Notice (2002)
programme.desc: An idealistic lawyer catches the attention of a real-estate tycoon, who offers her a job. She starts out thinking she can change his venal ways, but quickly tires of running his life for him. Finding himself in desperate need of her support, the self-absorbed millionaire pulls out all the stops to win her back. Romantic comedy, starring Sandra Bullock, Hugh Grant, Alicia Witt and Dana Ivey

programme.title: The Hangover (2009)
programme.desc: Three men on a stag weekend in Las Vegas wake up after a night of heavy drinking and find they have not only misplaced the groom, but somehow gained a tiger and a baby. Unable to remember the details of the night before, they set about piecing the clues together in the hope of finding their missing friend. Comedy, starring Bradley Cooper, Ed Helms and Zach Galifianakis

programme.title: American Pie 2 (2001)
programme.desc: The teenagers reunite after their first year at college to spend the summer at a lake house. Jim is concerned by his lack of sexual experience, and goes in search of an old flame, Oz struggles to keep a long-term relationship going, and Finch tries to master the art of tantric sex. Comedy sequel, starring Jason Biggs, Chris Klein, Seann William Scott, Alyson Hannigan and Eugene Levy

programme.title: American Pie: The Wedding (2003)
programme.desc: The friends from East Great Falls High reunite to celebrate Jim and Michelle's forthcoming wedding. Though reluctant to invite Stifler to their big day, the happy couple relent - but regret their decision when he plans a bachelor party on the same evening Jim is due to meet the in-laws. The third instalment in the comedy series, with Jason Biggs, Alyson Hannigan, Seann William Scott, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Eugene Levy

programme.title: Spanglish (2004)
programme.desc: A Mexican woman is offered a well-paid job as housekeeper to a wealthy American celebrity chef, and relocates with her daughter to Los Angeles - but her employer's neurotic wife causes all sorts of problems. Comedy directed by James L Brooks, starring Paz Vega, Adam Sandler, Tea Leoni and Cloris Leachman

programme.title: Superbad (2007)
programme.desc: Two teenage friends are on the brink of going to different colleges, so decide to mark the event by going to a party where they hope to lose their virginity. However, they have promised to supply the booze for the event, a task that proves much harder than expected because they are under age. Unfortunately, when a friend steps in to help, the situation gets even worse. Comedy, starring Jonah Hill, Michael Cera and Christopher Mintz-Plasse

programme.title: Zoolander (2001)
programme.desc: Dim male model Derek Zoolander is stunned to find his career going downhill thanks to the arrival of an equally vacuous rival - but fails to realise he is merely a pawn in a dangerous game of international espionage and world domination. Comedy, starring Ben Stiller, Owen Wilson, Will Ferrell, Milla Jovovich, Christine Taylor and David Duchovny

programme.title: National Security (2003)
programme.desc: Two men dismissed from the police become security guards, but are forced to go on the run when they uncover a sophisticated smuggling operation. With the criminal gang and the LAPD both chasing them, the unlikely partners seem more intent on fighting each other than covering their tracks. Action comedy, starring Martin Lawrence, Steve Zahn, Colm Feore and Eric Roberts

programme.title: Mean Machine (2001)
programme.desc: A disgraced soccer star jailed for assaulting two policemen is persuaded by the prison authorities to form a football team of inmates to take on the guards. Unfortunately, whipping his fellow prisoners into shape proves a tricky proposition. Comedy drama, starring Vinnie Jones, Jason Statham, David Hemmings and Jason Flemyng
